Defines the methods that will be available on the player\n */\nexport interface IGuiManager {\n  /**\n   * Show a text. This is a graphical interface already built. Opens the GUI named `rpg-dialog`\n   *\n   * ```ts\n   * player.showText('Hello World')\n   * ```\n   *\n   * The method returns a promise. It is resolved when the dialog box is closed.\n   *\n   * ```ts\n   * await player.showText('Hello World')\n   * // dialog box is closed, then ...\n   * ```\n   *\n   * **Option: position**\n   *\n   * You can define how the dialog box is displayed:\n   * - top\n   * - middle\n   * - bottom\n   *\n   * (bottom by default)\n   *\n   * ```ts\n   * player.showText('Hello World', {\n   *      position: 'top'\n   * })\n   * ```\n   *\n   * **Option: fullWidth**\n   *\n   * `boolean` (true by default)\n   *\n   * Indicate that the dialog box will take the full width of the screen.\n   *\n   * ```ts\n   * player.showText('Hello World', {\n   *      fullWidth: true\n   * })\n   * ```\n   *\n   * **Option: autoClose**\n   *\n   * `boolean` (false by default)\n   *\n   * If false, the user will have to press Enter to close the dialog box.\n   *\n   *  ```ts\n   * player.showText('Hello World', {\n   *      autoClose: true\n   * })\n   * ```\n   *\n   * **Option: typewriterEffect**\n   *\n   * `boolean` (true by default)\n   *\n   * Performs a typewriter effect\n   *\n   *  ```ts\n   * player.showText('Hello World', {\n   *      typewriterEffect: false\n   * })\n   * ```\n   *\n   * **Option: talkWith**\n   *\n   * `RpgPlayer` (nothing by default)\n   *\n   * If you specify the event or another player, the other player will stop his or her movement and look in the player's direction.\n   *\n   *  ```ts\n   * // Code in an event\n   * player.showText('Hello World', {\n   *      talkWith: this\n   * })\n   * ```\n   *\n   * @title Show Text\n   * @method player.showText(text,options)\n   * @param {string} text\n   * @param {object} [options] the different options, see usage below\n   * @returns {Promise}\n   * @memberof GuiManager\n   */\n  showText(msg: string, options?: DialogOptions): Promise<any>;\n\n  /**\n   * Shows a dialog box with a choice. Variables are key-value\n * pairs that can store any type of data associated with the player, such as\n * quest progress, game flags, inventory state, and custom game data.\n *\n * Player variables have two main roles:\n *\n * 1. Persist player-specific state so it can be restored from saves.\n * 2. Carry that state across maps and map servers through the player snapshot.\n * \n * @param Base - The base class to extend with variable management\n * @returns Extended class with variable management methods\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * class MyPlayer extends WithVariableManager(BasePlayer) {\n *   constructor() {\n *     super();\n *     // Variables are automatically initialized\n *   }\n * }\n * \n * const player = new MyPlayer();\n * player.setVariable('questCompleted', true);\n * ```\n */\nexport function WithVariableManager<TBase extends PlayerCtor>(Base: TBase) {\n  return class extends Base {\n    variables = type(signal<Record<string, any>>({}) as any, 'variables', { persist: true }, this as any);\n\n    setVariable(key: string, val: any): void {\n      this.variables.mutate((variables) => {\n        variables[key] = val;\n      });\n    }\n\n    getVariable<U = any>(key: string): U | undefined {\n      return this.variables()[key];\n    }\n\n    removeVariable(key: string): boolean {\n      const variables = this.variables();\n      if (!(key in variables)) {\n        return false;\n      }\n      this.variables.mutate((draft) => {\n        delete draft[key];\n      });\n      return true;\n    }\n\n    hasVariable(key: string): boolean {\n      return key in this.variables();\n    }\n\n    getVariableKeys(): string[] {\n      return Object.keys(this.variables());\n    }\n\n    clearVariables(): void {\n      this.variables.set({});\n    }\n  } as unknown as TBase;\n}\n\n/**\n * Interface for Variable Manager functionality\n * \n * Provides variable management capabilities including storing, retrieving, and managing\n * key-value pairs for player-specific data. This interface defines the public API\n * of the VariableManager mixin.\n */\nexport interface IVariableManager {\n  /**\n   * Map storing all player variables.\n   *\n   * These values belong to the player, are persisted, and travel with the\n   * player snapshot when switching maps or servers.\n   */\n  variables: Map<string, any>;\n\n  /**\n   * Assign a variable to the player.\n   *\n   * Use player variables for quest flags, per-player event state, and any value\n   * that must survive saves and map transitions.\n   * \n   * @param key - The variable identifier\n   * @param val - The value to store\n   * @memberof VariableManager\n   */\n  setVariable(key: string, val: any): void;\n\n  /**\n   * Get a variable value\n   * \n   * @param key - The variable identifier to retrieve\n   * @returns The stored value or undefined if not found\n   */\n  getVariable<U = any>(key: string): U | undefined;\n\n  /**\n   * Remove a variable\n   * \n   * @param key - The variable identifier to remove\n   * @returns true if a variable existed and has been removed, false otherwise\n   */\n  removeVariable(key: string): boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * Check if a variable exists\n   * \n   * @param key - The variable identifier to check\n   * @returns true if the variable exists, false otherwise\n   */\n  hasVariable(key: string): boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * Get all variable keys\n   * \n   * @returns Array of all variable keys\n   */\n  getVariableKeys(): string[];\n\n  /**\n   * Clear all variables\n   */\n  clearVariables(): void;\n}\n","import { isString, PlayerCtor } from \"@rpgjs/common\";\nimport { signal, computed, WritableSignal, ComputedSignal } from \"@signe/reactive\";\nimport { MAXHP, MAXSP } from \"@rpgjs/common\";\nimport { type } from \"@signe/sync\";\n\nexport type ExpCurve = {\n  basis: number;\n  extra: number;\n  accelerationA: number;\n  accelerationB: number;\n};\n\nexport type ParameterCurve = {\n  start: number;\n  end: number;\n};\n\nexport type ParameterValue = number | ParameterCurve;\n\nconst DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE: ExpCurve = {\n  basis: 30,\n  extra: 20,\n  accelerationA: 30,\n  accelerationB: 30\n};\n\nfunction isObject(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {\n  return typeof value === \"object\" && value !== null;\n}\n\nfunction toValidNumber(value: unknown, fallback: number): number {\n  return typeof value === \"number\" && Number.isFinite(value) ? value : fallback;\n}\n\nfunction normalizeExpCurve(value: unknown): ExpCurve {\n  if (!isObject(value)) return DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE;\n\n  return {\n    basis: toValidNumber(value.basis, DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE.basis),\n    extra: toValidNumber(value.extra, DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE.extra),\n    accelerationA: toValidNumber(value.accelerationA, DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE.accelerationA),\n    accelerationB: toValidNumber(value.accelerationB, DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE.accelerationB)\n  };\n}\n\nfunction normalizeParameterCurve(value: unknown): ParameterCurve {\n  if (typeof value === \"number\") {\n    const normalized = toValidNumber(value, 0);\n    return {\n      start: normalized,\n      end: normalized\n    };\n  }\n\n  if (!isObject(value)) {\n    return {\n      start: 0,\n      end: 0\n    };\n  }\n\n  const start = toValidNumber(value.start, 0);\n  const end = toValidNumber(value.end, start);\n\n  return {\n    start,\n    end\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Interface for Parameter Manager functionality\n * \n * Provides comprehensive parameter management including health points (HP), skill points (SP),\n * experience and level progression, custom parameters, and parameter modifiers.\n */\nexport interface IParameterManager {\n  /** \n   * ```ts\n   * player.initialLevel = 5\n   * ``` \n   * \n   * @title Set initial level\n   * @prop {number} player.initialLevel\n   * @default 1\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   * */\n  initialLevel: number;\n\n  /** \n   * ```ts\n   * player.finalLevel = 50\n   * ``` \n   * \n   * @title Set final level\n   * @prop {number} player.finalLevel\n   * @default 99\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   * */\n  finalLevel: number;\n\n  /** \n   * With Object-based syntax, you can use following options:\n   * - `basis: number`\n   * - `extra: number`\n   * - `accelerationA: number`\n   * - `accelerationB: number`\n   * @title Change Experience Curve\n   * @prop {object} player.expCurve\n   * @default \n   *  ```ts\n   * {\n   *      basis: 30,\n   *      extra: 20,\n   *      accelerationA: 30,\n   *      accelerationB: 30\n   * }\n   * ```\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   * */\n  expCurve: ExpCurve;\n\n  /** \n   * Changes the health points\n   * - Cannot exceed the MaxHP parameter\n   * - Cannot have a negative value\n   * - If the value is 0, a hook named `onDead()` is called in the RpgPlayer class.\n   * \n   * ```ts\n   * player.hp = 100\n   * ``` \n   * @title Change HP\n   * @prop {number} player.hp\n   * @default MaxHPValue\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   * */\n  hp: number;\n\n  /** \n   * Changes the skill points\n   * - Cannot exceed the MaxSP parameter\n   * - Cannot have a negative value\n   * \n   * ```ts\n   * player.sp = 200\n   * ``` \n   * @title Change SP\n   * @prop {number} player.sp\n   * @default MaxSPValue\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   * */\n  sp: number;\n\n  /** \n   * Changing the player's experience. \n   * ```ts\n   * player.exp += 100\n   * ```\n   * \n   * Levels are based on the experience curve.\n   * \n   * ```ts\n   * console.log(player.level) // 1\n   * console.log(player.expForNextlevel) // 150\n   * player.exp += 160\n   * console.log(player.level) // 2\n   * ```\n   * \n   * @title Change Experience\n   * @prop {number} player.exp\n   * @default 0\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   * */\n  exp: number;\n\n  /** \n   * Changing the player's level. \n   * \n   * ```ts\n   * player.level += 1\n   * ``` \n   * \n   * The level will be between the initial level given by the `initialLevel` and final level given by `finalLevel`\n   * \n   * ```ts\n   * player.finalLevel = 50\n   * player.level = 60 \n   * console.log(player.level) // 50\n   * ```\n   * \n   * @title Change Level\n   * @prop {number} player.level\n   * @default 1\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   * */\n  level: number;\n\n  /** \n   * ```ts\n   * console.log(player.expForNextlevel) // 150\n   * ```\n   * @title Experience for next level ?\n   * @prop {number} player.expForNextlevel\n   * @readonly\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   * */\n  readonly expForNextlevel: number;\n\n  /** \n   * Read the value of a parameter. Put the name of the parameter.\n   * \n   * ```ts\n   * import { Presets } from '@rpgjs/server'\n   * \n   * const { MAXHP } = Presets \n   * \n   * console.log(player.param[MAXHP])\n   * ```\n   * \n   * > Possible to use the `player.getParamValue(name)` method instead\n   * @title Get Param Value\n   * @prop {object} player.param\n   * @readonly\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   * */\n  readonly param: { [key: string]: number };\n\n  /** \n   * Direct parameter modifiers (reactive signal)\n   * \n   * > It is important that these parameters have been created beforehand with the `addParameter()` method.\n   * > By default, the following settings have been created: \n   * - maxhp\n   * - maxsp\n   * - str\n   * - int\n   * - dex\n   * - agi\n   * \n   * **Object Key**\n   * \n   * The key of the object is the name of the parameter\n   * \n   * > The good practice is to retrieve the name coming from a constant\n   * \n   * **Object Value**\n   * \n   * The value of the key is an object containing: \n   * ``` \n   * {\n   *   value: number,\n   *   rate: number\n   * }\n   * ```\n   * \n   * - value: Adds a number to the parameter\n   * - rate: Adds a rate to the parameter\n   * \n   * > Note that you can put both (value and rate)\n   * \n   * This property uses reactive signals - changes automatically trigger parameter recalculation.\n   * The final parameter values in `param` include aggregated modifiers from equipment, states, etc.\n   * \n   * @prop {Object} [paramsModifier]\n   * @example\n   * \n   * ```ts\n   * import { Presets } from '@rpgjs/server'\n   * \n   * const { MAXHP } = Presets\n   * \n   * // Set direct modifiers (reactive)\n   * player.paramsModifier = {\n   *      [MAXHP]: {\n   *          value: 100\n   *      }\n   * }\n   * \n   * // Parameters automatically recalculate\n   * console.log(player.param[MAXHP]); // Updated value\n   * ```\n   * \n   * @title Set Parameters Modifier\n   * @prop {object} paramsModifier\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   * */\n  paramsModifier: { \n    [key: string]: {\n      value?: number,\n      rate?: number\n    }\n  };\n\n  /**\n   * Get or set the parameters object\n   * \n   * @prop {object} parameters\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   */\n  parameters: { [key: string]: { start: number, end: number } };\n\n  /**\n   * Set a parameter with either a fixed value or a level curve\n   *\n   * A numeric value is stored as a fixed parameter where `start === end`.\n   *\n   * @param name - Parameter name\n   * @param value - Fixed value or parameter curve\n   */\n  setParameter(name: string, value: ParameterValue): void;\n\n  /**\n   * Get the value of a specific parameter by name\n   * \n   * @deprecated Use `player.param[name]` instead for better reactivity\n   * @param name - The name of the parameter to get\n   * @returns The calculated parameter value\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * import { Presets } from '@rpgjs/server'\n   * \n   * const { MAXHP } = Presets\n   * \n   * // Preferred way (reactive)\n   * const maxHp = player.param[MAXHP];\n   * \n   * // Legacy way (still works)\n   * const maxHp = player.getParamValue(MAXHP);\n   * ```\n   */\n  getParamValue(name: string): number;\n\n  /** \n   * Give a new parameter. Give a start value and an end value. \n   * The start value will be set to the level set at `player.initialLevel` and the end value will be linked to the level set at `player.finalLevel`.\n   * \n   * ```ts\n   * const SPEED = 'speed'\n   * \n   * player.addParameter(SPEED, {\n   *     start: 10,\n   *     end: 100\n   * })\n   * \n   * player.param[SPEED] // 10\n   * player.level += 5\n   * player.param[SPEED] // 14\n   * ```\n   * \n   * @title Add custom parameters\n   * @method player.addParameter(name,curve)\n   * @param {string} name - The name of the parameter\n   * @param {object} curve - Scheme of the object: { start: number, end: number }\n   * @returns {void}\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   * */\n  addParameter(name: string, curve: { start: number, end: number }): void;\n\n  /** \n   * Gives back in percentage of health points to skill points\n   * \n   * ```ts\n   * import { Presets } from '@rpgjs/server'\n   * \n   * const { MAXHP } = Presets \n   * \n   * console.log(player.param[MAXHP]) // 800\n   * player.hp = 100\n   * player.recovery({ hp: 0.5 }) // = 800 * 0.5\n   * console.log(player.hp) // 400\n   * ```\n   * \n   * @title Recovery HP and/or SP\n   * @method player.recovery(params)\n   * @param {object} params - Scheme of the object: { hp: number, sp: number }. The values of the numbers must be in 0 and 1\n   * @returns {void}\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   * */\n  recovery(params: { hp?: number, sp?: number }): void;\n\n  /** \n   * restores all HP and SP\n   * \n   * ```ts\n   * import { Presets } from '@rpgjs/server'\n   * \n   * const { MAXHP, MAXSP } = Presets \n   * \n   * console.log(player.param[MAXHP], player.param[MAXSP]) // 800, 230\n   * player.hp = 100\n   * player.sp = 0\n   * player.allRecovery()\n   * console.log(player.hp, player.sp) // 800, 230\n   * ```\n   * \n   * @title All Recovery\n   * @method player.allRecovery()\n   * @returns {void}\n   * @memberof ParameterManager\n   * */\n  allRecovery(): void;\n}\n\n/**\n * Parameter Manager Mixin with Reactive Signals\n * \n * Provides comprehensive parameter management functionality using reactive signals from `@signe/reactive`.\n * This mixin handles health points (HP), skill points (SP), experience and level progression, \n * custom parameters, and parameter modifiers with automatic reactivity.\n * \n * **Key Features:**\n * - ✨ **Reactive Parameters**: All parameters automatically recalculate when level or modifiers change\n * - 🚀 **Performance Optimized**: Uses computed signals to avoid unnecessary recalculations  \n * - 🔄 **Real-time Updates**: Changes propagate automatically throughout the system\n * - 🎯 **Type Safe**: Full TypeScript support with proper type inference\n * \n * @template TBase - The base class constructor type\n * @param Base - The base class to extend with parameter management\n * @returns Extended class with reactive parameter management methods\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * class MyPlayer extends WithParameterManager(BasePlayer) {\n *   constructor() {\n *     super();\n *     \n *     // Add custom parameters\n *     this.addParameter('strength', { start: 10, end: 100 });\n *     this.addParameter('magic', { start: 5, end: 80 });\n *   }\n * }\n * \n * const player = new MyPlayer();\n * \n * // Reactive parameter updates\n * player.level = 5;\n * console.log(player.param.strength); // Automatically calculated for level 5\n * \n * // Reactive modifiers\n * player.paramsModifier = {\n *   [MAXHP]: { value: 100, rate: 1.2 }\n * };\n * console.log(player.param[MAXHP]); // Automatically includes modifiers\n * ```\n */\nexport function WithParameterManager<TBase extends PlayerCtor>(Base: TBase) {\n  return class extends Base {\n    /**\n     * Signal for parameter modifiers - allows reactive updates when modifiers change\n     * \n     * This signal tracks temporary parameter modifications from equipment, states, etc.\n     * When updated, it automatically triggers recalculation of all computed parameters.\n     * \n     * @example\n     * ```ts\n     * // Set modifier that adds 100 to MaxHP\n     * player.paramsModifier = {\n     *   [MAXHP]: { value: 100 }\n     * };\n     * \n     * // Parameters automatically recalculate\n     * console.log(player.param[MAXHP]); // Updated value\n     * ```\n     */\n    private _paramsModifierSignal = type(signal<{\n        [key: string]: {\n            value?: number,\n            rate?: number\n        }\n    }>({}) as any, '_paramsModifierSignal', { persist: true }, this as any)\n\n    /**\n     * Signal for base parameters configuration\n     * \n     * Stores the start and end values for each parameter's level curve.\n     * Changes to this signal trigger recalculation of all parameter values.\n     */\n    private _parametersSignal = type(signal<{\n        [key: string]: {\n            start: number,\n            end: number\n        }\n    }>({}) as any, '_parametersSignal', { persist: true }, this as any)\n\n    private _paramProxy: { [key: string]: number } | null = null\n\n    /**\n     * Computed signal for all parameter values\n     * \n     * Automatically recalculates all parameter values when level or modifiers change.\n     * This provides reactive parameter updates throughout the system.\n     * \n     * @example\n     * ```ts\n     * // Access reactive parameters\n     * const maxHp = player.param[MAXHP]; // Always current value\n     * \n     * // Parameters update automatically when level changes\n     * player.level = 10;\n     * console.log(player.param[MAXHP]); // New calculated value\n     * ```\n     */\n    _param = type(computed(() => {\n        const obj = {}\n        const parameters = this._parametersSignal()\n        const allModifiers = this._getAggregatedModifiers()\n        const level = this._level()\n        \n        for (const [name, paramConfig] of Object.entries(parameters)) {\n            let curveVal = Math.floor((paramConfig.end - paramConfig.start) * ((level - 1) / (this.finalLevel - this.initialLevel))) + paramConfig.start\n            \n            const modifier = allModifiers[name]\n            if (modifier) {\n                if (modifier.rate) curveVal *= modifier.rate\n                if (modifier.value) curveVal += modifier.value\n            }\n            \n            obj[name] = curveVal\n        }\n        \n        return obj\n    }) as any, '_param', {}, this as any) \n\n    /**\n     * Aggregates parameter modifiers from all sources (direct modifiers, states, equipment)\n     * \n     * This method combines modifiers from multiple sources and calculates the final\n     * modifier values for each parameter. It handles both value and rate modifiers.\n     * \n     * @returns Aggregated parameter modifiers\n     * \n     * @example\n     * ```ts\n     * // Internal usage - gets modifiers from all sources\n     * const modifiers = this._getAggregatedModifiers();\n     * console.log(modifiers[MAXHP]); // { value: 100, rate: 1.2 }\n     * ```\n     */\n    private _getAggregatedModifiers(): { [key: string]: { value?: number, rate?: number } } {\n        const params = {}\n        const paramsAvg = {}\n        \n        const changeParam = (paramsModifier) => {\n            for (let key in paramsModifier) {\n                const { rate, value } = paramsModifier[key]\n                if (!params[key]) params[key] = { rate: 0, value: 0 }\n                if (!paramsAvg[key]) paramsAvg[key] = 0\n                if (value) params[key].value += value\n                if (rate !== undefined) params[key].rate += rate\n                paramsAvg[key]++\n            }\n        }\n        \n        const getModifier = (prop) => {\n            if (!isString(prop)) {\n                changeParam(prop)\n                return\n            }\n            for (let el of this[prop]()) {\n                if (!el.paramsModifier) continue\n                changeParam(el.paramsModifier)\n            }\n        }\n        \n        // Aggregate modifiers from all sources\n        getModifier(this._paramsModifierSignal())\n        getModifier('states')\n        getModifier('equipments')\n        \n        // Average the rates\n        for (let key in params) {\n            params[key].rate /= paramsAvg[key]\n        }\n        \n        return params\n    }\n\n     /** \n     * ```ts\n     * player.initialLevel = 5\n     * ``` \n     * \n     * @title Set initial level\n     * @prop {number} player.initialLevel\n     * @default 1\n     * @memberof ParameterManager\n     * */\n    public initialLevel:number = 1\n\n    /** \n     * ```ts\n     * player.finalLevel = 50\n     * ``` \n     * \n     * @title Set final level\n     * @prop {number} player.finalLevel\n     * @default 99\n     * @memberof ParameterManager\n     * */\n    public finalLevel:number = 99\n\n    /** \n     * With Object-based syntax, you can use following options:\n     * - `basis: number`\n     * - `extra: number`\n     * - `accelerationA: number`\n     * - `accelerationB: number`\n     * @title Change Experience Curve\n     * @prop {object} player.expCurve\n     * @default \n     *  ```ts\n     * {\n     *      basis: 30,\n     *      extra: 20,\n     *      accelerationA: 30,\n     *      accelerationB: 30\n     * }\n     * ```\n     * @memberof ParameterManager\n     * */\n    public _expCurveSignal = type(signal<string>(JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE)) as any, '_expCurveSignal', { persist: true }, this as any)\n\n    get expCurve(): ExpCurve { \n        const raw = this._expCurveSignal()\n        if (!raw) return DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE\n\n        try {\n            return normalizeExpCurve(JSON.parse(raw))\n        }\n        catch {\n            return DEFAULT_EXP_CURVE\n        }\n    }\n\n    set expCurve(val: ExpCurve) {\n        this._expCurveSignal.set(JSON.stringify(val)) \n    }\n    \n    /** \n     * Changes the health points\n     * - Cannot exceed the MaxHP parameter\n     * - Cannot have a negative value\n     * - If the value is 0, a hook named `onDead()` is called in the RpgPlayer class.\n     * \n     * ```ts\n     * player.hp = 100\n     * ``` \n     * @title Change HP\n     * @prop {number} player.hp\n     * @default MaxHPValue\n     * @memberof ParameterManager\n     * */\n    set hp(val: number) {\n        if (val > this.param[MAXHP]) {\n            val = this.param[MAXHP]\n        }\n        else if (val <= 0) {\n            this['execMethod']('onDead') \n            val = 0\n        }\n        this.hpSignal.set(val)\n    }\n\n    get hp(): number {\n        return this.hpSignal()\n    }\n\n    /** \n     * Changes the skill points\n     * - Cannot exceed the MaxSP parameter\n     * - Cannot have a negative value\n     * \n     * ```ts\n     * player.sp = 200\n     * ``` \n     * @title Change SP\n     * @prop {number} player.sp\n     * @default MaxSPValue\n     * @memberof ParameterManager\n     * */\n    set sp(val: number) {\n        if (val > this.param[MAXSP]) {\n            val = this.param[MAXSP]\n        }\n        this.spSignal.set(val)\n    }\n\n    get sp(): number {\n        return this.spSignal()\n    }   \n\n    /** \n     * Changing the player's experience. \n     * ```ts\n     * player.exp += 100\n     * ```\n     * \n     * Levels are based on the experience curve.\n     * \n     * ```ts\n     * console.log(player.level) // 1\n     * console.log(player.expForNextlevel) // 150\n     * player.exp += 160\n     * console.log(player.level) // 2\n     * ```\n     * \n     * @title Change Experience\n     * @prop {number} player.exp\n     * @default 0\n     * @memberof ParameterManager\n     * */\n    set exp(val: number) {\n        this._exp.set(val)\n        const lastLevel = this.level\n        while (this.expForNextlevel < this._exp()) {\n            this.level += 1\n        }\n        //const hasNewLevel = player.level - lastLevel\n    }\n\n    get exp(): number {\n        return this._exp()\n    }\n\n    /** \n     * Changing the player's level. \n     * \n     * ```ts\n     * player.level += 1\n     * ``` \n     * \n     * The level will be between the initial level given by the `initialLevel` and final level given by `finalLevel`\n     * \n     * ```ts\n     * player.finalLevel = 50\n     * player.level = 60 \n     * console.log(player.level) // 50\n     * ```\n     * \n     * @title Change Level\n     * @prop {number} player.level\n     * @default 1\n     * @memberof ParameterManager\n     * */\n    set level(val: number) {\n        const lastLevel = this._level()\n        if (this.finalLevel && val > this.finalLevel) {\n            val = this.finalLevel\n        }\n        const currentClass = this._class && this._class()\n        if (currentClass && 'skillsToLearn' in currentClass && Array.isArray(currentClass.skillsToLearn)) {\n            for (let i = this._level() ; i <= val; i++) {\n                for (let skill of currentClass.skillsToLearn as any[]) {\n                    if (skill.level == i) {\n                        this['learnSkill'](skill.skill)\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        const hasNewLevel = val - lastLevel\n        if (hasNewLevel > 0) {\n            this['execMethod']('onLevelUp', <any>[hasNewLevel])   \n        }\n        this._level.set(val)\n    }\n\n    get level(): number {\n        return this._level()\n    }\n     /** \n     * ```ts\n     * console.log(player.expForNextlevel) // 150\n     * ```\n     * @title Experience for next level ?\n     * @prop {number} player.expForNextlevel\n     * @readonly\n     * @memberof ParameterManager\n     * */\n    get expForNextlevel(): number {\n        return this._expForLevel(this.level + 1)\n    }\n\n    /** \n     * Read the value of a parameter. Put the name of the parameter.\n     * \n     * ```ts\n     * import { Presets } from '@rpgjs/server'\n     * \n     * const { MAXHP } = Presets \n     * \n     * console.log(player.param[MAXHP])\n     * ```\n     * \n     * > Possible to use the `player.getParamValue(name)` method instead\n     * @title Get Param Value\n     * @prop {object} player.param\n     * @readonly\n     * @memberof ParameterManager\n     * */\n    get param() {\n        if (!this._paramProxy) {\n            this._paramProxy = new Proxy({}, {\n                get: (_target, property) => {\n                    if (typeof property !== 'string') {\n                        return undefined\n                    }\n                    return this._param()[property]\n                },\n                set: (_target, property, value) => {\n                    if (typeof property !== 'string') {\n                        return false\n                    }\n                    this.setParameter(property, value as ParameterValue)\n                    return true\n                },\n                has: (_target, property) => {\n                    if (typeof property !== 'string') {\n                        return false\n                    }\n                    return property in this._param()\n                },\n                ownKeys: () => Reflect.ownKeys(this._param()),\n                getOwnPropertyDescriptor: (_target, property) => {\n                    if (typeof property !== 'string') {\n                        return undefined\n                    }\n                    const parameters = this._param()\n                    if (!(property in parameters)) {\n                        return undefined\n                    }\n                    return {\n                        configurable: true,\n                        enumerable: true,\n                        writable: true,\n                        value: parameters[property]\n                    }\n                }\n            }) as { [key: string]: number }\n        }\n        return this._paramProxy\n    }\n\n    get paramsModifier() {\n        return this._paramsModifierSignal()\n    }\n\n    /** \n     * Changes the values of some parameters\n     * \n     * > It is important that these parameters have been created beforehand with the `addParameter()` method.\n     * > By default, the following settings have been created: \n     * - maxhp\n     * - maxsp\n     * - str\n     * - int\n     * - dex\n     * - agi\n     * \n     * **Object Key**\n     * \n     * The key of the object is the name of the parameter\n     * \n     * > The good practice is to retrieve the name coming from a constant\n     * \n     * **Object Value**\n     * \n     * The value of the key is an object containing: \n     * ``` \n     * {\n     *   value: number,\n     *   rate: number\n     * }\n     * ```\n     * \n     * - value: Adds a number to the parameter\n     * - rate: Adds a rate to the parameter\n     * \n     * > Note that you can put both (value and rate)\n     * \n     * In the case of a state or the equipment of a weapon or armor, the parameters will be changed but if the state disappears or the armor/weapon is de-equipped, then the parameters will return to the initial state.\n     * \n     * @prop {Object} [paramsModifier]\n     * @example\n     * \n     * ```ts\n     * import { Presets } from '@rpgjs/server'\n     * \n     * const { MAXHP } = Presets\n     * \n     * player.paramsModifier = {\n     *      [MAXHP]: {\n     *          value: 100\n     *      }\n     * }\n     * ```\n     * \n     * 1. Player has 741 MaxHp\n     * 2. After changing the parameter, he will have 841 MaxHp\n     * \n     * @title Set Parameters Modifier\n     * @prop {number} paramsModifier\n     * @memberof ParameterManager\n     * */\n    set paramsModifier(val: { \n        [key: string]: {\n            value?: number,\n            rate?: number\n        }\n    }) {\n        this._paramsModifierSignal.set(val)\n    }\n\n    get parameters() {\n        return this._parametersSignal()\n    }\n\n    set parameters(val) {\n        const normalizedParameters = {}\n        for (const [name, parameterValue] of Object.entries(val || {})) {\n            normalizedParameters[name] = normalizeParameterCurve(parameterValue)\n        }\n        this._parametersSignal.set(normalizedParameters)\n        if (MAXHP in normalizedParameters && this.hp > this.param[MAXHP]) {\n            this.hp = this.param[MAXHP]\n        }\n        if (MAXSP in normalizedParameters && this.sp > this.param[MAXSP]) {\n            this.sp = this.param[MAXSP]\n        }\n    }\n\n    private _expForLevel(level: number): number {\n        const {\n            basis,\n            extra,\n            accelerationA,\n            accelerationB\n        } = this.expCurve\n        return Math.round(basis * (Math.pow(level - 1, 0.9 + accelerationA / 250)) * level * (level + 1) / (6 + Math.pow(level, 2) / 50 / accelerationB) + (level - 1) * extra)\n    }\n\n    getParamValue(name: string): number | never {\n        return this.param[name]\n    }\n\n    /** \n     * Give a new parameter. Give a start value and an end value. \n     * The start value will be set to the level set at `player.initialLevel` and the end value will be linked to the level set at `player.finalLevel`.\n     * \n     * ```ts\n     * const SPEED = 'speed'\n     * \n     * player.addParameter(SPEED, {\n     *     start: 10,\n     *     end: 100\n     * })\n     * \n     * player.param[SPEED] // 10\n     * player.level += 5\n     * player.param[SPEED] // 14\n     * ```\n     * \n     * @title Add custom parameters\n     * @method player.addParameter(name,curve)\n     * @param {string} name - The name of the parameter\n     * @param {object} curve - Scheme of the object: { start: number, end: number }\n     * @returns {void}\n     * @memberof ParameterManager\n     * */\n    setParameter(name: string, value: ParameterValue): void {\n        const normalizedValue = normalizeParameterCurve(value)\n        this._parametersSignal.mutate(parameters => {\n            parameters[name] = {\n                start: normalizedValue.start,\n                end: normalizedValue.end\n            }\n        })\n        const maxHp = this.param[MAXHP]\n        const maxSp = this.param[MAXSP]\n        if (name == MAXHP && this.hp > maxHp) {\n            this.hp = maxHp // forcing hp not to exceed maxp \n        }\n        else if (name == MAXSP && this.sp > maxSp) {\n            this.sp = maxSp\n        }\n    }\n\n    addParameter(name: string, { start, end }: { start: number, end: number }): void {\n        this.setParameter(name, { start, end })\n    }\n\n    /** \n     * Gives back in percentage of health points to skill points\n     * \n     * ```ts\n     * import { Presets } from '@rpgjs/server'\n     * \n     * const { MAXHP } = Presets \n     * \n     * console.log(player.param[MAXHP]) // 800\n     * player.hp = 100\n     * player.recovery({ hp: 0.5 }) // = 800 * 0.5\n     * console.log(player.hp) // 400\n     * ```\n     * \n     * @title Recovery HP and/or SP\n     * @method player.recovery(params)\n     * @param {object} params - Scheme of the object: { hp: number, sp: number }. The values of the numbers must be in 0 and 1\n     * @returns {void}\n     * @memberof ParameterManager\n     * */\n    recovery({ hp, sp }: { hp?: number, sp?: number }) {\n        if (hp) this.hp = this.param[MAXHP] * hp\n        if (sp) this.sp = this.param[MAXSP] * sp\n    }\n\n    /** \n     * restores all HP and SP\n     * \n     * ```ts\n     * import { Presets } from '@rpgjs/server'\n     * \n     * const { MAXHP, MAXSP } = Presets \n     * \n     * console.log(player.param[MAXHP], player.param[MAXSP]) // 800, 230\n     * player.hp = 100\n     * player.sp = 0\n     * player.allRecovery()\n     * console.log(player.hp, player.sp) // 800, 230\n     * ```\n     * \n     * @title All Recovery\n     * @method player.allRecovery()\n     * @returns {void}\n     * @memberof ParameterManager\n     * */\n    allRecovery(): void {\n        this.recovery({ hp: 1, sp: 1 })\n    }\n  } as unknown as TBase;\n}\n","import { ItemInstance } from \"@rpgjs/database\";\nimport { PlayerCtor } from \"@rpgjs/common\";\n\nexport function WithItemFixture<TBase extends PlayerCtor>(\n  Base: TBase\n) {\n  return class extends Base {\n    protected isInstanceOf, isString, Item, type PlayerCtor} from \"@rpgjs/common\";\nimport { ATK, PDEF, SDEF } from \"@rpgjs/common\";\nimport { ItemLog } from \"../logs\";\nimport type { ItemClass, ItemInstance } from \"@rpgjs/database\";\nimport { RpgPlayer } from \"./Player\";\n\n// Ajout des enums manquants\nenum Effect {\n  CAN_NOT_ITEM = 'CAN_NOT_ITEM'\n}\n\nenum ClassHooks {\n  canEquip = 'canEquip'\n}\n\ntype Inventory = { nb: number; item: ItemInstance };\n\n/**\n * Interface defining the hooks that can be implemented on item classes or objects\n * \n * These hooks are called at specific moments during the item lifecycle:\n * - `onAdd`: When the item is added to the player's inventory\n * - `onUse`: When the item is successfully used\n * - `onUseFailed`: When the item usage fails (e.g., chance roll failed)\n * - `onRemove`: When the item is removed from the inventory\n * - `onEquip`: When the item is equipped or unequipped\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * const itemHooks: ItemHooks = {\n *   onAdd(player) {\n *     console.log('Item added to inventory');\n *   },\n *   onUse(player) {\n *     player.hp += 100;\n *   }\n * };\n * ```\n */\nexport interface ItemHooks {\n  /**\n   * Called when the item is added to the player's inventory\n   * \n   * @param player - The player receiving the item\n   */\n  onAdd?: (player: RpgPlayer) => void | Promise<void>;\n\n  /**\n   * Called when the item is successfully used\n   * \n   * @param player - The player using the item\n   */\n  onUse?: (player: RpgPlayer) => void | Promise<void>;\n\n  /**\n   * Called when the item usage fails (e.g., chance roll failed)\n   * \n   * @param player - The player attempting to use the item\n   */\n  onUseFailed?: (player: RpgPlayer) => void | Promise<void>;\n\n  /**\n   * Called when the item is removed from the inventory\n   * \n   * @param player - The player losing the item\n   */\n  onRemove?: (player: RpgPlayer) => void | Promise<void>;\n\n  /**\n   * Called when the item is equipped or unequipped\n   * \n   * @param player - The player equipping/unequipping the item\n   * @param equip - true if equipping, false if unequipping\n   */\n  onEquip?: (player: RpgPlayer, equip: boolean) => void | Promise<void>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Base properties that can be included in an item object\n * \n * This interface defines the common properties that items can have.\n * Use this as a base and extend it with specific item types.\n * \n * @template T - Additional properties specific to the item type\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * interface PotionData extends ItemData {\n *   hpValue: number;\n *   mpValue: number;\n * }\n * \n * const potion: ItemObject<PotionData> = {\n *   name: 'Health Potion',\n *   description: 'Restores 100 HP',\n *   price: 200,\n *   hpValue: 100,\n *   mpValue: 0,\n *   onUse(player) {\n *     player.hp += this.hpValue;\n *   }\n * };\n * ```\n */\nexport interface ItemData {\n  /** Item name */\n  name?: string;\n  /** Item description */\n  description?: string;\n  /** Item price */\n  price?: number;\n  /** HP value restored when used */\n  hpValue?: number;\n  /** MP/SP value restored when used */\n  mpValue?: number;\n  /** Chance to successfully use the item (0-1) */\n  hitRate?: number;\n  /** Whether the item is consumable */\n  consumable?: boolean;\n  /** States to add when used */\n  addStates?: any[];\n  /** States to remove when used */\n  removeStates?: any[];\n  /** Elemental properties */\n  elements?: any[];\n  /** Parameter modifiers */\n  paramsModifier?: Record<string, any>;\n  /** Item type (for equipment validation) */\n  _type?: 'item' | 'weapon' | 'armor';\n}\n\n/**\n * Item object type that combines data properties with hooks\n * \n * This type allows you to create item objects directly without needing a class.\n * The object can contain both item data properties and lifecycle hooks.\n * \n * @template T - Additional properties specific to the item type (extends ItemData)\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * const potion: ItemObject = {\n *   name: 'Health Potion',\n *   description: 'Restores 100 HP',\n *   price: 200,\n *   hpValue: 100,\n *   consumable: true,\n *   onAdd(player) {\n *     console.log('Potion added!');\n *   },\n *   onUse(player) {\n *     player.hp += 100;\n *   }\n * };\n * \n * player.addItem(potion);\n * ```\n */\nexport type ItemObject<T extends ItemData = ItemData> = T & ItemHooks & {\n  /** Item identifier (required if not using class or string) */\n  id?: string;\n};\n\n/**\n * Item Manager Mixin\n *\n * Provides comprehensive item management capabilities to any class. This mixin handles\n * inventory management, item usage, equipment, buying/selling, and item effects.\n * It manages the complete item system including restrictions, transactions, and equipment.\n *\n * @param Base - The base class to extend with item management\n * @returns Extended class with item management methods\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * class MyPlayer extends WithItemManager(BasePlayer) {\n *   constructor() {\n *     super();\n *     // Item system is automatically initialized\n *   }\n * }\n * \n * const player = new MyPlayer();\n * player.addItem('potion', 5);\n * player.useItem('potion');\n * ```\n */\nexport function WithItemManager<TBase extends PlayerCtor>(Base: TBase) {\n  return class extends Base {\n\n    getItem(itemClass: ItemClass | string): Item {\n      const index: number = this._getItemIndex(itemClass);\n      return (this as any).items()[index];\n    }\n\n    hasItem(itemClass: ItemClass | string): boolean {\n      return !!this.getItem(itemClass);\n    }\n\n    _getItemIndex(itemClass: ItemClass | string): number {\n      return (this as any).items().findIndex((it: Item): boolean => {\n        if (isString(itemClass)) {\n          return it.id() == itemClass;\n        }\n        return isInstanceOf(it, itemClass);\n      });\n    }\n\n    private _getItemMap(required: boolean = true) {\n      // Use this.map directly to support both RpgMap and LobbyRoom\n      const map = (this as any).getCurrentMap?.() || (this as any).map;\n      if (required && (!map || !map.database)) {\n        throw new Error('Player must be on a map to add items');\n      }\n      return map;\n    }\n\n    private _resolveItemInput(\n      item: ItemClass | ItemObject | string,\n      map: any,\n      databaseByIdOverride?: (id: string) => any\n    ) {\n      let itemId: string;\n      let data: any;\n      let itemInstance: any = null;\n\n      if (isString(item)) {\n        itemId = item as string;\n        data = databaseByIdOverride\n          ? databaseByIdOverride(itemId)\n          : (this as any).databaseById(itemId);\n      } else if (typeof item === 'function' || (item as any).prototype) {\n        itemId = (item as any).name;\n\n        const existingData = map.database()[itemId];\n        if (existingData) {\n          data = existingData;\n        } else {\n          map.addInDatabase(itemId, item as ItemClass);\n          data = item as ItemClass;\n        }\n\n        itemInstance = new (item as ItemClass)();\n      } else {\n        const itemObj = item as ItemObject;\n        itemId = itemObj.id || `item-${Date.now()}`;\n\n        const existingData = map.database()[itemId];\n        if (existingData) {\n          data = { ...existingData, ...itemObj };\n          map.addInDatabase(itemId, data, { force: true });\n        } else {\n          map.addInDatabase(itemId, itemObj);\n          data = itemObj;\n        }\n\n        itemInstance = itemObj;\n      }\n\n      return { itemId, data, itemInstance };\n    }\n\n    private _createItemInstance(\n      itemId: string,\n      data: any,\n      nb: number,\n      itemInstance: any\n    ): Item {\n      const instance = new Item(data);\n      instance.id.set(itemId);\n      instance.quantity.set(nb);\n\n      if (itemInstance) {\n        (instance as any)._itemInstance = itemInstance;\n        if (itemInstance.onAdd) {\n          instance.onAdd = itemInstance.onAdd.bind(itemInstance);\n        }\n      }\n\n      return instance;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Create an item instance without inventory changes or hook execution.\n     */\n    createItemInstance(item: ItemClass | ItemObject | string, nb: number = 1) {\n      const map = this._getItemMap();\n      const { itemId, data, itemInstance } = this._resolveItemInput(item, map);\n      const instance = this._createItemInstance(itemId, data, nb, itemInstance);\n      return { itemId, data, itemInstance, instance };\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Resolve item snapshot entries into Item instances without side effects.\n     */\n    resolveItemsSnapshot(snapshot: { items?: any[] }, mapOverride?: any) {\n      if (!snapshot || !Array.isArray(snapshot.items)) {\n        return snapshot;\n      }\n\n      const map = mapOverride ?? this._getItemMap(false);\n      if (!map || !map.database) {\n        return snapshot;\n      }\n\n      const databaseByIdOverride = (id: string) => {\n        const data = map.database()[id];\n        if (!data) {\n          throw new Error(\n            `The ID=${id} data is not found in the database. Add the data in the property \"database\"`\n          );\n        }\n        return data;\n      };\n\n      const items = snapshot.items.map((entry: any) => {\n        const itemId = isString(entry) ? entry : entry?.id;\n        if (!itemId) {\n          return entry;\n        }\n\n        const nb =\n          !isString(entry) && typeof entry?.nb === 'number'\n            ? entry.nb\n            : !isString(entry) && typeof entry?.quantity === 'number'\n              ? entry.quantity\n              : 1;\n\n        const { data, itemInstance } = this._resolveItemInput(\n          itemId,\n          map,\n          databaseByIdOverride\n        );\n        return this._createItemInstance(itemId, data, nb, itemInstance);\n      });\n\n      return { ...snapshot, items };\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Resolve equipment snapshot entries into Item instances without side effects.\n     */\n    resolveEquipmentsSnapshot(snapshot: { equipments?: any[]; items?: any[] }, mapOverride?: any) {\n      if (!snapshot || !Array.isArray(snapshot.equipments)) {\n        return snapshot;\n      }\n\n      const map = mapOverride ?? this._getItemMap(false);\n      if (!map || !map.database) {\n        return snapshot;\n      }\n\n      const databaseByIdOverride = (id: string) => {\n        const data = map.database()[id];\n        if (!data) {\n          throw new Error(\n            `The ID=${id} data is not found in the database. Add the data in the property \"database\"`\n          );\n        }\n        return data;\n      };\n\n      const resolvedItems = Array.isArray(snapshot.items) ? snapshot.items : [];\n      const getItemId = (entry: any) => {\n        if (isString(entry)) return entry;\n        if (typeof entry?.id === 'function') return entry.id();\n        return entry?.id;\n      };\n\n      const equipments = snapshot.equipments.map((entry: any) => {\n        const itemId = getItemId(entry);\n        if (!itemId) {\n          return entry;\n        }\n\n        const existing = resolvedItems.find((item: any) => {\n          const existingId = getItemId(item);\n          return existingId === itemId;\n        });\n        if (existing) {\n          return existing;\n        }\n\n        const { data, itemInstance } = this._resolveItemInput(\n          itemId,\n          map,\n          databaseByIdOverride\n        );\n        return this._createItemInstance(itemId, data, 1, itemInstance);\n      });\n\n      return { ...snapshot, equipments };\n    }\n    addItem(item: ItemClass | ItemObject | string, nb: number = 1): Item {\n      const map = this._getItemMap();\n      const { itemId, data, itemInstance } = this._resolveItemInput(item, map);\n\n      // Find existing item in inventory\n      const existingItem = (this as any).items().find((it: Item) => it.id() == itemId);\n      let instance: Item;\n\n      if (existingItem) {\n        // Item already exists, update quantity and merge properties\n        instance = existingItem;\n        instance.quantity.update((it) => it + nb);\n        \n        // Update item properties from merged data (e.g., name, description, price)\n        if (data.name !== undefined) {\n          instance.name.set(data.name);\n        }\n        if (data.description !== undefined) {\n          instance.description.set(data.description);\n        }\n        if (data.price !== undefined) {\n          instance.price.set(data.price);\n        }\n        \n        // Update stored instance if it's an object with hooks\n        if (itemInstance && typeof itemInstance === 'object' && !(itemInstance instanceof Function)) {\n          (instance as any)._itemInstance = itemInstance;\n          // Update hooks if they exist\n          if (itemInstance.onAdd) {\n            instance.onAdd = itemInstance.onAdd.bind(itemInstance);\n          }\n        }\n      } else {\n        // Create new item instance\n        instance = this._createItemInstance(itemId, data, nb, itemInstance);\n        (this as any).items().push(instance);\n      }\n\n      // Call onAdd hook - use stored instance if available\n      const hookTarget = (instance as any)._itemInstance || instance;\n      // Only call onAdd if it exists and is a function\n      (this as any)[\"execMethod\"](\"onAdd\", [this], hookTarget);\n      return instance;\n    }\n\n    removeItem(\n      itemClass: ItemClass | string,\n      nb: number = 1\n    ): Item | undefined {\n      const itemIndex: number = this._getItemIndex(itemClass);\n      if (itemIndex == -1) {\n        throw ItemLog.notInInventory(itemClass);\n      }\n      const currentNb: number = this.items()[itemIndex].quantity();\n      const item = this.items()[itemIndex];\n      if (currentNb - nb <= 0) {\n        this.items().splice(itemIndex, 1);\n      } else {\n        this.items()[itemIndex].quantity.update((it) => it - nb);\n      }\n      // Call onRemove hook - use stored instance if available\n      const hookTarget = (item as any)._itemInstance || item;\n      if (hookTarget && typeof hookTarget.onRemove === 'function') {\n        this[\"execMethod\"](\"onRemove\", [this], hookTarget);\n      }\n      return this.items()[itemIndex];\n    }\n\n    buyItem(item: ItemClass | ItemObject | string, nb = 1): Item {\n      let itemId: string;\n      let data: any;\n      \n      if (isString(item)) {\n        itemId = item as string;\n        data = (this as any).databaseById(itemId);\n      } else if (typeof item === 'function' || (item as any).prototype) {\n        itemId = (item as any).name;\n        data = (this as any).databaseById(itemId);\n      } else {\n        const itemObj = item as ItemObject;\n        itemId = itemObj.id || `item-${Date.now()}`;\n        try {\n          const dbData = (this as any).databaseById(itemId);\n          data = { ...dbData, ...itemObj };\n        } catch {\n          data = itemObj;\n        }\n      }\n      \n      if (!data.price) {\n        throw ItemLog.haveNotPrice(itemId);\n      }\n      const totalPrice = nb * data.price;\n      if (this._gold() < totalPrice) {\n        throw ItemLog.notEnoughGold(itemId, nb);\n      }\n      this._gold.update((gold) => gold - totalPrice);\n      return this.addItem(item, nb);\n    }\n\n    sellItem(itemClass: ItemClass | string, nbToSell = 1): Item {\n      const itemId = isString(itemClass) ? itemClass : (itemClass as any).name;\n      const data = (this as any).databaseById(itemId);\n      const inventory = this.getItem(itemClass);\n      if (!inventory) {\n        throw ItemLog.notInInventory(itemId);\n      }\n      const quantity = inventory.quantity();\n      if (quantity - nbToSell < 0) {\n        throw ItemLog.tooManyToSell(itemId, nbToSell, quantity);\n      }\n      if (!data.price) {\n        throw ItemLog.haveNotPrice(itemId);\n      }\n      this._gold.update((gold) => gold + (data.price / 2) * nbToSell);\n      this.removeItem(itemClass, nbToSell);\n      return inventory;\n    }\n\n    getParamItem(name: string): number {\n      let nb = 0;\n      for (let item of this.equipments()) {\n        // Retrieve item data from database to get properties like atk, pdef, sdef\n        try {\n          const itemData = (this as any).databaseById(item.id());\n          nb += itemData[name] || 0;\n        } catch {\n          // If item not in database, skip it\n        }\n      }\n      const modifier = (this as any).paramsModifier?.[name];\n      if (modifier) {\n        if (modifier.value) nb += modifier.value;\n        if (modifier.rate) nb *= modifier.rate;\n      }\n      return nb;\n    }\n\n    get atk(): number {\n      return this.getParamItem(ATK);\n    }\n\n    get pdef(): number {\n      return this.getParamItem(PDEF);\n    }\n\n    get sdef(): number {\n      return this.getParamItem(SDEF);\n    }\n\n    useItem(itemClass: ItemClass | string): Item {\n      const itemId = isString(itemClass) ? itemClass : (itemClass as any).name;\n      const inventory = this.getItem(itemClass);\n      if ((this as any).hasEffect?.(Effect.CAN_NOT_ITEM)) {\n        throw ItemLog.restriction(itemId);\n      }\n      if (!inventory) {\n        throw ItemLog.notInInventory(itemId);\n      }\n      \n      // Retrieve item data from database to check consumable and hitRate\n      const itemData = (this as any).databaseById(itemId);\n      const consumable = itemData?.consumable;\n      \n      // If consumable is explicitly false, throw error\n      if (consumable === false) {\n        throw ItemLog.notUseItem(itemId);\n      }\n      \n      // If consumable is undefined and item is not of type 'item', it's not consumable\n      if (consumable === undefined && itemData?._type && itemData._type !== 'item') {\n        throw ItemLog.notUseItem(itemId);\n      }\n      \n      const hitRate = itemData?.hitRate ?? 1;\n      const hookTarget = (inventory as any)._itemInstance || inventory;\n      \n      if (Math.random() > hitRate) {\n        this.removeItem(itemClass);\n        this[\"execMethod\"](\"onUseFailed\", [this], hookTarget);\n        throw ItemLog.chanceToUseFailed(itemId);\n      }\n      (this as any).applyEffect?.(itemData);\n      (this as any).applyStates?.(this, itemData);\n      this[\"execMethod\"](\"onUse\", [this], hookTarget);\n      this.removeItem(itemClass);\n      return inventory;\n    }\n\n    equip(\n      itemId: string,\n      equip: boolean | 'auto' = true\n    ): void {\n      const autoAdd = equip === 'auto';\n      const equipState = equip === 'auto' ? true : equip;\n      const data = (this as any).databaseById(itemId);\n      let inventory: Item = this.getItem(itemId);\n      if (!inventory && autoAdd) {\n        inventory = this.addItem(itemId, 1);\n      }\n      if (!inventory) {\n        throw ItemLog.notInInventory(itemId);\n      }\n      if (data._type == \"item\") {\n        throw ItemLog.invalidToEquiped(itemId);\n      }\n\n      if (this._class && this._class()[ClassHooks.canEquip]) {\n        const canEquip = this[\"execMethodSync\"](\n          ClassHooks.canEquip,\n          [inventory, this],\n          this._class()\n        );\n        if (!canEquip) {\n          throw ItemLog.canNotEquip(itemId);\n        }\n      }\n\n      const item = inventory;\n\n      if ((item as any).equipped && equipState) {\n        throw ItemLog.isAlreadyEquiped(itemId);\n      }\n      (item as any).equipped = equipState;\n      if (!equipState) {\n        const index = this.equipments().findIndex((it) => it.id() == item.id());\n        this.equipments().splice(index, 1);\n      } else {\n        this.equipments().push(item);\n      }\n      // Call onEquip hook - use stored instance if available\n      const hookTarget = (item as any)._itemInstance || item;\n      this[\"execMethod\"](\"onEquip\", [this, equipState], hookTarget);\n    }\n  } as unknown as TBase;\n}\n\n/**\n * Interface for Item Manager functionality\n * \n * Provides comprehensive item management capabilities including inventory management,\n * item usage, equipment, buying/selling, and item effects. This interface defines\n * the public API of the ItemManager mixin.\n */\nexport interface IItemManager {\n  /**\n   * Retrieves the information of an object: the number and the instance\n   * \n   * The returned Item instance contains the quantity information accessible via `quantity()` method.\n   * \n   * @param itemClass - Item class or string identifier. If string, it's the item ID\n   * @returns Item instance containing quantity and item data\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * import Potion from 'your-database/potion'\n   * \n   * player.addItem(Potion, 5)\n   * const inventory = player.getItem(Potion)\n   * console.log(inventory.quantity()) // 5\n   * console.log(inventory) // <instance of Item>\n   * ```\n   */\n  getItem(itemClass: ItemClass | string): Item;\n\n  /**\n   * Check if the player has the item in his inventory\n   * \n   * @param itemClass - Item class or string identifier. If string, it's the item ID\n   * @returns `true` if player has the item, `false` otherwise\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * import Potion from 'your-database/potion'\n   * \n   * player.hasItem(Potion) // false\n   * player.addItem(Potion, 1)\n   * player.hasItem(Potion) // true\n   * ```\n   */\n  hasItem(itemClass: ItemClass | string): boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * Add an item in the player's inventory\n   * \n   * You can add items using:\n   * - Item class (automatically registered in database if needed)\n   * - Item object (automatically registered in database if needed)\n   * - String ID (must be pre-registered in database)\n   * \n   * The `onAdd()` method is called on the ItemClass or ItemObject when the item is added.\n   * \n   * @param item - Item class, object, or string identifier\n   * @param nb - Number of items to add (default: 1)\n   * @returns The item instance added to inventory\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * import Potion from 'your-database/potion'\n   * \n   * // Add using class\n   * player.addItem(Potion, 5)\n   * \n   * // Add using string ID (must be registered in database)\n   * player.addItem('Potion', 3)\n   * \n   * // Add using object\n   * player.addItem({\n   *   id: 'custom-potion',\n   *   name: 'Custom Potion',\n   *   price: 200,\n   *   onAdd(player) {\n   *     console.log('Custom potion added!')\n   *   }\n   * }, 2)\n   * ```\n   */\n  addItem(item: ItemClass | ItemObject | string, nb?: number): Item;\n\n  /**\n   * Deletes an item from inventory\n   * \n   * Decreases the quantity by `nb`. If the quantity falls to 0 or below, the item is removed from the inventory.\n   * The method returns `undefined` if the item is completely removed.\n   * \n   * The `onRemove()` method is called on the ItemClass when the item is removed.\n   * \n   * @param itemClass - Item class or string identifier. If string, it's the item ID\n   * @param nb - Number of items to remove (default: 1)\n   * @returns Item instance or `undefined` if the item was completely removed\n   * @throws {Object} ItemLog.notInInventory - If the item is not in the inventory\n   *   - `id`: `ITEM_NOT_INVENTORY`\n   *   - `msg`: Error message\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * import Potion from 'your-database/potion'\n   * \n   * try {\n   *   player.removeItem(Potion, 5)\n   * } catch (err) {\n   *   console.log(err) // { id: 'ITEM_NOT_INVENTORY', msg: '...' }\n   * }\n   * ```\n   */\n  removeItem(itemClass: ItemClass | string, nb?: number): Item | undefined;\n\n  /**\n   * Purchases an item and reduces the amount of gold\n   * \n   * The player's gold is reduced by `nb * item.price`. The item is then added to the inventory.\n   * The `onAdd()` method is called on the ItemClass when the item is added.\n   * \n   * @param item - Item class, object, or string identifier\n   * @param nb - Number of items to buy (default: 1)\n   * @returns Item instance added to inventory\n   * @throws {Object} ItemLog.haveNotPrice - If the item has no price set\n   *   - `id`: `NOT_PRICE`\n   *   - `msg`: Error message\n   * @throws {Object} ItemLog.notEnoughGold - If the player doesn't have enough gold\n   *   - `id`: `NOT_ENOUGH_GOLD`\n   *   - `msg`: Error message\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * import Potion from 'your-database/potion'\n   * \n   * try {\n   *   player.buyItem(Potion)\n   * } catch (err) {\n   *   if (err.id === 'NOT_ENOUGH_GOLD') {\n   *     console.log('Not enough gold!')\n   *   } else if (err.id === 'NOT_PRICE') {\n   *     console.log('Item has no price!')\n   *   }\n   * }\n   * ```\n   */\n  buyItem(item: ItemClass | ItemObject | string, nb?: number): Item;\n\n  /**\n   * Sell an item and the player wins the amount of the item divided by 2\n   * \n   * The player receives `(item.price / 2) * nbToSell` gold. The item is removed from the inventory.\n   * The `onRemove()` method is called on the ItemClass when the item is removed.\n   * \n   * @param itemClass - Item class or string identifier. If string, it's the item ID\n   * @param nbToSell - Number of items to sell (default: 1)\n   * @returns Item instance that was sold\n   * @throws {Object} ItemLog.haveNotPrice - If the item has no price set\n   *   - `id`: `NOT_PRICE`\n   *   - `msg`: Error message\n   * @throws {Object} ItemLog.notInInventory - If the item is not in the inventory\n   *   - `id`: `ITEM_NOT_INVENTORY`\n   *   - `msg`: Error message\n   * @throws {Object} ItemLog.tooManyToSell - If trying to sell more items than available\n   *   - `id`: `TOO_MANY_ITEM_TO_SELL`\n   *   - `msg`: Error message\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * import Potion from 'your-database/potion'\n   * \n   * try {\n   *   player.addItem(Potion)\n   *   player.sellItem(Potion)\n   * } catch (err) {\n   *   console.log(err)\n   * }\n   * ```\n   */\n  sellItem(itemClass: ItemClass | string, nbToSell?: number): Item;\n\n  /**\n   * Use an object. Applies effects and states. Removes the object from the inventory\n   * \n   * When an item is used:\n   * - Effects are applied to the player (HP/MP restoration, etc.)\n   * - States are applied/removed as defined in the item\n   * - The item is removed from inventory (consumed)\n   * \n   * If the item has a `hitRate` property (0-1), there's a chance the usage might fail.\n   * If usage fails, the item is still removed and `onUseFailed()` is called instead of `onUse()`.\n   * \n   * The `onUse()` method is called on the ItemClass if the use was successful.\n   * The `onUseFailed()` method is called on the ItemClass if the chance roll failed.\n   * The `onRemove()` method is called on the ItemClass when the item is removed.\n   * \n   * @param itemClass - Item class or string identifier. If string, it's the item ID\n   * @returns Item instance that was used\n   * @throws {Object} ItemLog.restriction - If the player has the `Effect.CAN_NOT_ITEM` effect\n   *   - `id`: `RESTRICTION_ITEM`\n   *   - `msg`: Error message\n   * @throws {Object} ItemLog.notInInventory - If the item is not in the inventory\n   *   - `id`: `ITEM_NOT_INVENTORY`\n   *   - `msg`: Error message\n   * @throws {Object} ItemLog.notUseItem - If the item's `consumable` property is `false`\n   *   - `id`: `NOT_USE_ITEM`\n   *   - `msg`: Error message\n   * @throws {Object} ItemLog.chanceToUseFailed - If the chance to use the item failed (hitRate roll failed)\n   *   - `id`: `USE_CHANCE_ITEM_FAILED`\n   *   - `msg`: Error message\n   *   - Note: The item is still deleted from the inventory even if usage failed\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * import Potion from 'your-database/potion'\n   * \n   * try {\n   *   player.addItem(Potion)\n   *   player.useItem(Potion)\n   * } catch (err) {\n   *   if (err.id === 'USE_CHANCE_ITEM_FAILED') {\n   *     console.log('Item usage failed due to chance roll')\n   *   } else {\n   *     console.log(err)\n   *   }\n   * }\n   * ```\n   */\n  useItem(itemClass: ItemClass | string): Item;\n\n  /**\n   * Equips a weapon or armor on a player\n   * \n   * Think first to add the item in the sdef: number;\n}\n","import {\n  arrayFlat,\n  arrayUniq,\n  Constructor,\n  PlayerCtor,\n  RpgCommonPlayer,\n} from \"@rpgjs/common\";\n\nexport enum Effect {\n  CAN_NOT_SKILL = 'CAN_NOT_SKILL',\n 'SUPER_GUARD'\n}\n\n/**\n * Effect Manager Mixin\n * \n * Provides effect management capabilities to any class. This mixin handles\n * player effects including restrictions, buffs, and debuffs. Effects can come\n * from various sources like states, equipment, and temporary conditions.\n * \n * @param Base - The base class to extend with effect management\n * @returns Extended class with effect management methods\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * class MyPlayer extends WithEffectManager(BasePlayer) {\n *   constructor() {\n *     super();\n *     // Effect system is automatically initialized\n *   }\n * }\n * \n * const player = new MyPlayer();\n * player.effects = [Effect.GUARD];\n * console.log(player.hasEffect(Effect.GUARD)); // true\n * ```\n */\nexport function WithEffectManager<TBase extends PlayerCtor>(Base: TBase) {\n  return class extends Base {\n    hasEffect(effect: string): boolean {\n      return this.effects.includes(effect);\n    }\n\n    get effects(): any[] {\n      const getEffects = (prop) => {\n        return arrayFlat(this[prop]().map((el) => el.effects || []));\n      };\n      return arrayUniq([\n        ...this._effects(),\n        ...getEffects(\"states\"),\n        ...getEffects(\"equipments\"),\n      ]);\n    }\n\n    set effects(val) {\n      this._effects.set(val);\n    }\n  } as unknown as TBase;\n}\n\n/**\n * Interface for Effect Manager functionality\n * \n * Provides effect management capabilities including restrictions, buffs, and debuffs.\n * This interface defines the public API of the EffectManager mixin.\n */\nexport interface IEffectManager {\n  /**\n   * Gets all currently active effects on the player from multiple sources:\n   * -
