b[PDEF] / 2) * ((20 + a[STR]) / 20))\n    if (damage < 0) damage = 0\n    return damage\n}\n\nexport const DAMAGE_GUARD = function(damage) {\n    return damage / 2\n}\n\nexport const COEFFICIENT_ELEMENTS = function(a, b, bDef) {\n    return ((a.rate + 1) * (b.rate + 1)) / (bDef.rate == 0 ? bDef.rate * 4 : 1)\n}\n\nexport const DAMAGE_SKILL = function(a, b, skill) {\n    let power = skill.power + (a[ATK] * (skill.coefficient[ATK] || 0))\n    if (power > 0) {\n        power -= b[PDEF] * (skill.coefficient[PDEF] || 0) / 2\n        power -= b[SDEF] * (skill.coefficient[SDEF] || 0) / 2\n        power = Math.max(power, 0)\n    }\n    let rate = 20;\n    [STR, DEX, AGI, INT].forEach(val => rate += a[val] * (skill.coefficient[val] || 0))\n    return Math.round(power * rate / 20)\n}","import { arrayUniq, PlayerCtor, RpgCommonPlayer } from \"@rpgjs/common\";\nimport { Constructor } from \"@rpgjs/common\";\nimport { RpgPlayer } from \"./Player\";\n\n/**\n * Element Manager Mixin\n * \n * Provides elemental management capabilities to any class. This mixin handles\n * elemental resistances, vulnerabilities, and attack elements. It manages both\n * defensive capabilities (elementsDefense) and offensive elements from equipment,\n * as well as player-specific elemental efficiency modifiers.\n * \n * @param Base - The base class to extend with element management\n * @returns Extended class with element management methods\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * class MyPlayer extends WithElementManager(BasePlayer) {\n *   constructor() {\n *     super();\n *     this.elementsEfficiency = [{ rate: 0.5, element: 'fire' }];\n *   }\n * }\n * \n * const player = new MyPlayer();\n * const fireResistance = player.elementsDefense.find(e => e.element === 'fire');\n * ```\n */\nexport function WithElementManager<TBase extends PlayerCtor>(Base: TBase) {\n  return class extends Base {\n    _elementsEfficiency: { rate: number; element: any }[] = [];\n\n    get elementsDefense(): { rate: number; element: any }[] {\n      return (this as any).getFeature(\"elementsDefense\", \"element\");\n    }\n\n    get elementsEfficiency(): { rate: number; element: any }[] {\n      if (this._class()) {\n        const classData = this._class() as any;\n        return <any>[\n          ...this._elementsEfficiency,\n          ...(classData?.elementsEfficiency || []),\n        ];\n      }\n      return this._elementsEfficiency;\n    }\n\n    set elementsEfficiency(val) {\n      this._elementsEfficiency = val;\n    }\n\n    get elements(): {\n      rate: number;\n      element: string;\n    }[] {\n      let elements: any = [];\n      for (let item of this.equipments()) {\n        if (item.elements) {\n          elements = [...elements, ...item.elements];\n        }\n      }\n      return arrayUniq(elements);\n    }\n\n    coefficientElements(otherPlayer: RpgPlayer): number {\n      const atkPlayerElements: any = (otherPlayer as any).elements;\n      const playerElements: any = this.elementsEfficiency;\n      let coefficient = 1;\n\n      for (let atkElement of atkPlayerElements) {\n        const elementPlayer = playerElements.find(\n          (el) => el.element == atkElement.element\n        );\n        const elementPlayerDef = this.elementsDefense.find(\n          (el) => el.element == atkElement.element\n        );\n        if (!elementPlayer) continue;\n        const fn = (this as any).getFormulas(\"coefficientElements\");\n        if (!fn) {\n          return coefficient;\n        }\n        coefficient += fn(\n          atkElement,\n          elementPlayer,\n          elementPlayerDef || { rate: 0 }\n        );\n      }\n      return coefficient;\n    }\n  } as unknown as TBase;\n}\n\n/**\n * Interface for Element Manager functionality\n * \n * Provides elemental management capabilities including resistances, vulnerabilities,\n * and attack elements. This interface defines the public API of the ElementManager mixin.\n */\nexport interface IElementManager {\n  /**\n   * Gets the defensive capabilities against various elements from equipped items.\n   * The system automatically consolidates multiple defensive items, keeping only\n   * the highest protection rate for each element type.\n   * \n   * @returns Array of element defense objects with rate and element properties\n   */\n  elementsDefense: { rate: number; element: any }[];\n\n  /**\n   * Manages the player's elemental efficiency modifiers, which determine how\n   * effective different elements are against this player. Values greater than 1\n   * indicate vulnerability, while values less than 1 indicate resistance.\n   * This combines both class-based efficiency and player-specific modifiers.\n   * \n   * @returns Array of element efficiency objects with rate and element properties\n   */\n  elementsEfficiency: { rate: number; element: any }[];\n\n  /**\n   * Gets all offensive elements available to the player from equipped weapons and armor.\n   * This determines what elemental damage types the player can deal in combat.\n   * The system automatically combines elements from all equipped items and removes duplicates.\n   * \n   * @returns Array of element objects with rate and element properties for offensive capabilities\n   */\n  elements: {\n    rate: number;\n    element: string;\n  }[];\n\n  /**\n   * Calculate elemental damage coefficient against another player\n   * \n   * Determines the damage multiplier when this player attacks another player,\n   * taking into account the attacker's offensive elements, the defender's\n   * elemental efficiency, and elemental defense from equipment. This is used\n   * in the battle system to calculate elemental damage modifiers.\n   * \n   * @param otherPlayer - The target player to calculate coefficient against\n   * @returns Numerical coefficient to multiply base damage by\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Calculate elemental damage coefficient\n   * const firePlayer = new MyPlayer();\n   * const icePlayer = new MyPlayer();\n   * \n   * // Fire player attacks ice player (assuming ice is weak to fire)\n   * const coefficient = icePlayer.coefficientElements(firePlayer);\n   * console.log(`Damage multiplier: ${coefficient}`); // e.g., 2.0 for double damage\n   * \n   * // Use in damage calculation\n   * const baseDamage = 100;\n   * const finalDamage = baseDamage * coefficient;\n   * console.log(`Final damage: ${finalDamage}`);\n   * \n   * // Check for elemental advantage\n   * if (coefficient > 1) {\n   *   console.log('Attacker has elemental advantage!');\n   * } else if (coefficient < 1) {\n   *   console.log('Defender resists this element');\n   * }\n   * ```\n   */\n  coefficientElements(otherPlayer: RpgPlayer): number;\n}","import {\n  isArray,\n  isInstanceOf,\n  isString,\n  PlayerCtor,\n  Skill,\n} from \"@rpgjs/common\";\nimport { SkillLog } from \"../logs\";\nimport { RpgPlayer } from \"./Player\";\nimport { Effect } from \"./EffectManager\";\n\n/**\n * Type for skill class constructor\n */\ntype SkillClass = { new (...args: any[]): any };\n\n/**\n * Interface defining the hooks that can be implemented on skill classes or objects\n * \n * These hooks are called at specific moments during the skill lifecycle:\n * - `onLearn`: When the skill is learned by the player\n * - `onUse`: When the skill is successfully used\n * - `onUseFailed`: When the skill usage fails (e.g., chance roll failed)\n * - `onForget`: When the skill is forgotten\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * const skillHooks: SkillHooks = {\n *   onLearn(player) {\n *     console.log('Skill learned!');\n *   },\n *   onUse(player, target) {\n *     console.log('Skill used on target');\n *   }\n * };\n * ```\n */\nexport interface SkillHooks {\n  /**\n   * Called when the skill is learned by the player\n   * \n   * @param player - The player learning the skill\n   */\n  onLearn?: (player: RpgPlayer) => void | Promise<void>;\n\n  /**\n   * Called when the skill is successfully used\n   * \n   * @param player - The player using the skill\n   * @param target - The target player(s) if any\n   */\n  onUse?: (player: RpgPlayer, target?: RpgPlayer | RpgPlayer[]) => void | Promise<void>;\n\n  /**\n   * Called when the skill usage fails (e.g., chance roll failed)\n   * \n   * @param player - The player attempting to use the skill\n   * @param target - The intended target player(s) if any\n   */\n  onUseFailed?: (player: RpgPlayer, target?: RpgPlayer | RpgPlayer[]) => void | Promise<void>;\n\n  /**\n   * Called when the skill is forgotten\n   * \n   * @param player - The player forgetting the skill\n   */\n  onForget?: (player: RpgPlayer) => void | Promise<void>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Interface for skill object definition\n * \n * Defines the properties that a skill can have when defined as an object.\n * Skills can be defined as objects, classes, or string IDs referencing the database.\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * const fireSkill: SkillObject = {\n *   id: 'fire',\n *   name: 'Fire',\n *   description: 'A basic fire spell',\n *   spCost: 10,\n *   hitRate: 0.9,\n *   power: 50,\n *   onUse(player) {\n *     console.log('Fire spell cast!');\n *   }\n * };\n * \n * player.learnSkill(fireSkill);\n * ```\n */\nexport interface SkillObject extends SkillHooks {\n  /**\n   * Unique identifier for the skill\n   * If not provided, one will be auto-generated\n   */\n  id?: string;\n\n  /**\n   * Display name of the skill\n   */\n  name?: string;\n\n  /**\n   * Description of the skill\n   */\n  description?: string;\n\n  /**\n   * SP (Skill Points) cost to use the skill\n   * @default 0\n   */\n  spCost?: number;\n\n  /**\n   * Hit rate (0-1) - probability of successful skill usage\n   * @default 1\n   */\n  hitRate?: number;\n\n  /**\n   * Base power of the skill for damage calculation\n   */\n  power?: number;\n\n  /**\n   * Coefficient multipliers for damage calculation\n   */\n  coefficient?: Record<string, number>;\n\n  /**\n   * Type marker for database\n   */\n  _type?: 'skill';\n\n  /**\n   * Allow additional properties\n   */\n  [key: string]: any;\n}\n\n/**\n * Skill Manager Mixin\n * \n * Provides skill management capabilities to any class. This mixin handles\n * learning, forgetting, and using skills, including SP cost management,\n * hit rate calculations, and skill effects application.\n * \n * Supports three input formats for skills:\n * - **String ID**: References a skill in the database\n * - **Class**: A skill class that will be instantiated\n * - **Object**: A skill object with properties and hooks\n * \n * @param Base - The base class to extend with skill management\n * @returns Extended class with skill management methods\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * // Using string ID (from database)\n * player.learnSkill('fire');\n * \n * // Using skill class\n * player.learnSkill(FireSkill);\n * \n * // Using skill object\n * player.learnSkill({\n *   id: 'ice',\n *   name: 'Ice',\n *   spCost: 15,\n *   onUse(player) {\n *     console.log('Ice spell cast!');\n *   }\n * });\n * ```\n */\nexport function WithSkillManager<TBase extends PlayerCtor>(Base: TBase): TBase {\n  return class extends (Base as any) {\n    private _getSkillSnapshot(skillData: any) {\n      if (!skillData) return null;\n\n      const snapshot = {\n        ...((skillData as any)._skillData || skillData),\n      };\n\n      const reactiveKeys = [\n        \"id\",\n        \"name\",\n        \"description\",\n        \"spCost\",\n        \"icon\",\n        \"hitRate\",\n        \"power\",\n        \"coefficient\",\n      ];\n\n      for (const key of reactiveKeys) {\n        const value = (skillData as any)[key];\n        if (typeof value === \"function\") {\n          if (key === \"hitRate\" && !(key in snapshot)) {\n            continue;\n          }\n          snapshot[key] = value();\n        } else if (value !== undefined) {\n          snapshot[key] = value;\n        }\n      }\n\n      if ((skillData as any)._skillInstance) {\n        snapshot._skillInstance = (skillData as any)._skillInstance;\n      }\n\n      return snapshot;\n    }\n\n    private _getLearnedSkillEntry(skillInput: SkillClass | SkillObject | string): Skill | null {\n      const index = this._getSkillIndex(skillInput);\n      return index >= 0 ? ((this as any).skills()[index] as Skill) : null;\n    }\n\n    private _getSkillMap(required: boolean = true) {\n      // Use this.map directly to support both RpgMap and LobbyRoom\n      const map = (this as any).getCurrentMap?.() || (this as any).map;\n      if (required && (!map || !map.database)) {\n        throw new Error('Player must be on a map to learn skills');\n      }\n      return map;\n    }\n\n    private _resolveSkillInput(\n      skillInput: SkillClass | SkillObject | string,\n      map: any,\n      databaseByIdOverride?: (id: string) => any\n    ) {\n      let skillId = '';\n      let skillData: any;\n      let skillInstance: any = null;\n\n      if (isString(skillInput)) {\n        skillId = skillInput as string;\n        skillData = databaseByIdOverride\n          ? databaseByIdOverride(skillId)\n          : (this as any).databaseById(skillId);\n      } else if (typeof skillInput === 'function') {\n        const SkillClassCtor = skillInput as SkillClass;\n        skillId = (SkillClassCtor as any).id || SkillClassCtor.name;\n\n        const existingData = map?.database()?.[skillId];\n        if (existingData) {\n          skillData = existingData;\n        } else if (map) {\n          map.addInDatabase(skillId, SkillClassCtor);\n          skillData = SkillClassCtor;\n        } else {\n          skillData = SkillClassCtor;\n        }\n\n        skillInstance = new SkillClassCtor();\n        skillData = { ...skillData, ...skillInstance, id: skillId };\n      } else {\n        const skillObj = skillInput as SkillObject;\n        skillId = skillObj.id || `skill-${Date.now()}`;\n        skillObj.id = skillId;\n\n        const existingData = map?.database()?.[skillId];\n        if (existingData) {\n          skillData = { ...existingData, ...skillObj };\n          if (map) {\n            map.addInDatabase(skillId, skillData, { force: true });\n          }\n        } else if (map) {\n          map.addInDatabase(skillId, skillObj);\n          skillData = skillObj;\n        } else {\n          skillData = skillObj;\n        }\n\n        skillInstance = skillObj;\n      }\n\n      return { skillId, skillData, skillInstance };\n    }\n\n    private _createSkillInstance(\n      skillId: string,\n      skillData: any,\n      skillInstance: any\n    ) {\n      const instance = new Skill(skillData);\n      instance.id.set(skillId);\n      (instance as any)._skillData = {\n        ...skillData,\n        id: skillId,\n      };\n\n      if (skillInstance) {\n        (instance as any)._skillInstance = skillInstance;\n      }\n\n      return instance;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Create a skill instance without learning side effects.\n     */\n    createSkillInstance(skillInput: SkillClass | SkillObject | string) {\n      const map = this._getSkillMap();\n      const { skillId, skillData, skillInstance } = this._resolveSkillInput(skillInput, map);\n      const instance = this._createSkillInstance(skillId, skillData, skillInstance);\n      return { skillId, skillData, skillInstance, instance };\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Resolve skill snapshot entries into Skill instances without side effects.\n     */\n    resolveSkillsSnapshot(snapshot: { skills?: any[] }, mapOverride?: any) {\n      if (!snapshot || !Array.isArray(snapshot.skills)) {\n        return snapshot;\n      }\n\n      const map = mapOverride ?? this._getSkillMap(false);\n      if (!map || !map.database) {\n        return snapshot;\n      }\n\n      const databaseByIdOverride = (id: string) => {\n        const data = map.database()[id];\n        if (!data) {\n          throw new Error(\n            `The ID=${id} data is not found in the database. Add the data in the property \"database\"`\n          );\n        }\n        return data;\n      };\n\n      const skills = snapshot.skills.map((entry: any) => {\n        const skillId = isString(entry) ? entry : entry?.id;\n        if (!skillId) {\n          return entry;\n        }\n\n        const { skillData, skillInstance } = this._resolveSkillInput(\n          skillId,\n          map,\n          databaseByIdOverride\n        );\n        return this._createSkillInstance(skillId, skillData, skillInstance);\n      });\n\n      return { ...snapshot, skills };\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Find the index of a skill in the skills array\n     * \n     * Searches by ID for both string inputs and object/class inputs.\n     * \n     * @param skillInput - Skill ID, class, or object to find\n     * @returns Index of the skill or -1 if not found\n     */\n    _getSkillIndex(skillInput: SkillClass | SkillObject | string): number {\n      // Get the ID to search for\n      let searchId = '';\n      \n      if (isString(skillInput)) {\n        searchId = skillInput as string;\n      } else if (typeof skillInput === 'function') {\n        // It's a class - use the class name as ID\n        searchId = (skillInput as any).id || skillInput.name;\n      } else {\n        // It's an object - use its id property\n        searchId = (skillInput as SkillObject).id || '';\n      }\n\n      return (this as any).skills().findIndex((skill: any) => {\n        const skillId = skill.id() || skill.name() || '';\n        return skillId === searchId;\n      });\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Retrieves a learned skill\n     * \n     * Searches the player's learned skills by ID, class, or object.\n     * \n     * @param skillInput - Skill ID, class, or object to find\n     * @returns The skill data if found, null otherwise\n     * \n     * @example\n     * ```ts\n     * const skill = player.getSkill('fire');\n     * if (skill) {\n     *   console.log(`Fire skill costs ${skill.spCost} SP`);\n     * }\n     * ```\n     */\n    getSkill(skillInput: SkillClass | SkillObject | string): Skill | null {\n      const skill = this._getLearnedSkillEntry(skillInput);\n      return this._getSkillSnapshot(skill) as Skill | null;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Learn a new skill\n     * \n     * Adds a skill to the player's skill list. Supports three input formats:\n     * - **String ID**: Retrieves the skill from the database\n     * - **Class**: Creates an instance and adds to database if needed\n     * - **Object**: Uses directly and adds to database if needed\n     * \n     * @param skillInput - Skill ID, class, or object to learn\n     * @returns The learned skill data\n     * @throws SkillLog.alreadyLearned if the skill is already known\n     * \n     * @example\n     * ```ts\n     * // From database\n     * player.learnSkill('fire');\n     * \n     * // From class\n     * player.learnSkill(FireSkill);\n     * \n     * // From object\n     * player.learnSkill({\n     *   id: 'custom-skill',\n     *   name: 'Custom Skill',\n     *   spCost: 20,\n     *   onLearn(player) {\n     *     console.log('Learned custom skill!');\n     *   }\n     * });\n     * ```\n     */\n    learnSkill(skillInput: SkillClass | SkillObject | string): any {\n      const map = this._getSkillMap();\n      const { skillId, skillData, skillInstance } = this._resolveSkillInput(skillInput, map);\n\n      // Check if already learned\n      if (this._getLearnedSkillEntry(skillId)) {\n        throw SkillLog.alreadyLearned(skillData);\n      }\n\n      const instance = this._createSkillInstance(skillId, skillData, skillInstance);\n\n      (this as any).skills().push(instance);\n      \n      // Call onLearn hook\n      const hookTarget = (instance as any)._skillInstance || instance;\n      this[\"execMethod\"](\"onLearn\", [this], hookTarget);\n      \n      return skillData;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Forget a learned skill\n     * \n     * Removes a skill from the player's skill list.\n     * \n     * @param skillInput - Skill ID, class, or object to forget\n     * @returns The forgotten skill data\n     * @throws SkillLog.notLearned if the skill is not known\n     * \n     * @example\n     * ```ts\n     * player.forgetSkill('fire');\n     * // or\n     * player.forgetSkill(FireSkill);\n     * ```\n     */\n    forgetSkill(skillInput: SkillClass | SkillObject | string): any {\n      const index = this._getSkillIndex(skillInput);\n      \n      if (index === -1) {\n        // Get skill data for error message\n        let skillData: any = skillInput;\n        if (isString(skillInput)) {\n          try {\n            skillData = (this as any).databaseById(skillInput);\n          } catch {\n            skillData = { name: skillInput, id: skillInput };\n          }\n        } else if (typeof skillInput === 'function') {\n          skillData = { name: (skillInput as SkillClass).name, id: (skillInput as any).id || (skillInput as SkillClass).name };\n        }\n        throw SkillLog.notLearned(skillData);\n      }\n      \n      const skillEntry = (this as any).skills()[index];\n      const skillData = this._getSkillSnapshot(skillEntry);\n      (this as any).skills().splice(index, 1);\n      \n      // Call onForget hook\n      const hookTarget =\n        (skillEntry as any)?._skillInstance ||\n        (skillEntry as any)?._skillData ||\n        skillData;\n      this[\"execMethod\"](\"onForget\", [this], hookTarget);\n      \n      return skillData;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Use a learned skill\n     * \n     * Executes a skill, consuming SP and applying effects to targets.\n     * The skill must be learned and the player must have enough SP.\n     * \n     * @param skillInput - Skill ID, class, or object to use\n     * @param otherPlayer - Optional target player(s) to apply skill effects to\n     * @returns The used skill data\n     * @throws SkillLog.restriction if player has CAN_NOT_SKILL effect\n     * @throws SkillLog.notLearned if skill is not known\n     * @throws SkillLog.notEnoughSp if not enough SP\n     * @throws SkillLog.chanceToUseFailed if hit rate check fails\n     * \n     * @example\n     * ```ts\n     * // Use skill without target\n     * player.useSkill('fire');\n     * \n     * // Use skill on a target\n     * player.useSkill('fire', enemy);\n     * \n     * // Use skill on multiple targets\n     * player.useSkill('fire', [enemy1, enemy2]);\n     * ```\n     */\n    useSkill(skillInput: SkillClass | SkillObject | string, otherPlayer?: RpgPlayer | RpgPlayer[]): any {\n      const skillEntry = this._getLearnedSkillEntry(skillInput);\n      const skill = this._getSkillSnapshot(skillEntry);\n      \n      // Check for skill restriction effect\n      if ((this as any).hasEffect(Effect.CAN_NOT_SKILL)) {\n        throw SkillLog.restriction(skill || skillInput);\n      }\n      \n      // Check if skill is learned\n      if (!skill) {\n        throw SkillLog.notLearned(skillInput);\n      }\n      \n      // Check SP cost\n      const spCost = typeof skill.spCost === \"number\" ? skill.spCost : 0;\n      if (spCost > (this as any).sp) {\n        throw SkillLog.notEnoughSp(skill, spCost, (this as any).sp);\n      }\n      \n      // Consume SP (halved if HALF_SP_COST effect is active)\n      const costMultiplier = (this as any).hasEffect(Effect.HALF_SP_COST) ? 2 : 1;\n      (this as any).sp -= spCost / costMultiplier;\n      \n      // Check hit rate\n      const hitRate = typeof skill.hitRate === \"number\" ? skill.hitRate : 1;\n      if (Math.random() > hitRate) {\n        const hookTarget =\n          (skillEntry as any)?._skillInstance ||\n          (skillEntry as any)?._skillData ||\n          skill;\n        this[\"execMethod\"](\"onUseFailed\", [this, otherPlayer], hookTarget);\n        throw SkillLog.chanceToUseFailed(skill);\n      }\n      \n      // Apply effects to targets\n      if (otherPlayer) {\n        const players: RpgPlayer[] = isArray(otherPlayer) ? otherPlayer as RpgPlayer[] : [otherPlayer as RpgPlayer];\n        for (const player of players) {\n          (this as any).applyStates(player, skill);\n          (player as any).applyDamage(this, skill);\n        }\n      }\n      \n      // Call onUse hook\n      const hookTarget =\n        (skillEntry as any)?._skillInstance ||\n        (skillEntry as any)?._skillData ||\n        skill;\n      this[\"execMethod\"](\"onUse\", [this, otherPlayer], hookTarget);\n      \n      return skill;\n    }\n  } as unknown as TBase;\n}\n\n/**\n * Interface for Skill Manager functionality\n * \n * Provides skill management capabilities including learning, forgetting, and using skills.\n * This interface defines the public API of the SkillManager mixin.\n */\nexport interface ISkillManager {\n  /**\n   * Retrieves a learned skill. Returns null if not found\n   * \n   * @param skillInput - Skill class, object, or data id\n   * @returns The skill data or null\n   */\n  getSkill(skillInput: SkillClass | SkillObject | string): any | null;\n\n  /**\n   * Learn a skill\n   * \n   * Supports three input formats:\n   * - String ID: Retrieves from database\n   * - Class: Creates instance and adds to database\n   * - Object: Uses directly and adds to database\n   * \n   * @param skillInput - Skill class, object, or data id\n   * @returns The learned skill data\n   * @throws SkillLog.alreadyLearned if the player already knows the skill\n   */\n  learnSkill(skillInput: SkillClass | SkillObject | string): any;\n\n  /**\n   * Forget a skill\n   * \n   * @param skillInput - Skill class, object, or data id\n   * @returns The forgotten skill data\n   * @throws SkillLog.notLearned if trying to forget a skill not learned\n   */\n  forgetSkill(skillInput: SkillClass | SkillObject | string): any;\n\n  /**\n   * Use a skill\n   * \n   * @param skillInput - Skill class, object, or data id\n   * @param otherPlayer - Optional target player(s) to apply skill to\n   * @returns The used skill data\n   * @throws SkillLog.restriction if player has Effect.CAN_NOT_SKILL\n   * @throws SkillLog.notLearned if player tries to use an unlearned skill\n   * @throws SkillLog.notEnoughSp if player does not have enough SP\n   * @throws SkillLog.chanceToUseFailed if the chance to use the skill has failed\n   */\n  useSkill(skillInput: SkillClass | SkillObject | string, otherPlayer?: RpgPlayer | RpgPlayer[]): any;\n}\n","import { Constructor, PlayerCtor, RpgCommonPlayer } from \"@rpgjs/common\";\nimport { RpgPlayer } from \"./Player\";\nimport { ATK, PDEF, SDEF } from \"@rpgjs/common\";\nimport { Effect } from \"./EffectManager\";\nimport type { IElementManager } from \"./ElementManager\";\nimport type { IEffectManager } from \"./EffectManager\";\nimport type { IParameterManager } from \"./ParameterManager\";\n\n/**\n * Interface combining methods from other managers needed by BattleManager\n * Reuses existing interfaces instead of duplicating method signatures\n */\ninterface PlayerWithMixins extends IElementManager, IEffectManager, Pick<IParameterManager, 'parameters' | 'hp'> {\n  getFormulas(name: string): any;\n  getCurrentMap(): ReturnType<RpgPlayer['getCurrentMap']>;\n}\n\nexport interface IBattleManager {\n   /**\n     * Apply damage. Player will lose HP. the `attackerPlayer` parameter is the other player, the one who attacks.\n     *\n     * If you don't set the skill parameter, it will be a physical attack.\n     * The attack formula is already defined but you can customize it in the server options.\n     * This method handles all aspects of damage calculation including critical hits,\n     * elemental vulnerabilities, guard effects, and applies the final damage to HP.\n     *\n     * @param attackerPlayer - The attacking player who deals the damage\n     * @param skill - Optional skill object for magical attacks, if not provided uses physical attack\n     * @returns Object containing damage details and special effects that occurred\n     * \n     * @example\n     * ```ts\n     * // Physical attack\n     * const result = player.applyDamage(attackerPlayer);\n     * console.log(`Physical damage: ${result.damage}, Critical: ${result.critical}`);\n     * \n     * // Magical attack with skill\n     * const fireSkill = { id: 'fire', power: 50, element: 'fire' };\n     * const magicResult = player.applyDamage(attackerPlayer, fireSkill);\n     * console.log(`Magic damage: ${magicResult.damage}, Vulnerable: ${magicResult.elementVulnerable}`);\n     * \n     * // Check for guard effects\n     * if (result.guard) {\n     *   console.log('Attack was partially blocked!');\n     * }\n     * if (result.superGuard) {\n     *   console.log('Attack was heavily reduced by super guard!');\n     * }\n     * ```\n     */\n  applyDamage(attackerPlayer: RpgPlayer, skill?: any): {\n    damage: number;\n    critical: boolean;\n    elementVulnerable: boolean;\n    guard: boolean;\n    superGuard: boolean;\n  };\n}\n\nexport function WithBattleManager<TBase extends PlayerCtor>(Base: TBase): new (...args: ConstructorParameters<TBase>) => InstanceType<TBase> & IBattleManager {\n  return class extends Base {\n    applyDamage(\n      attackerPlayer: RpgPlayer,\n      skill?: any\n    ): {\n      damage: number;\n      critical: boolean;\n      elementVulnerable: boolean;\n      guard: boolean;\n      superGuard: boolean;\n    } {\n      const self = this as unknown as PlayerWithMixins;\n      const getParam = (player: RpgPlayer) => {\n        const params = {};\n        Object.keys(self.parameters).forEach((key) => {\n          params[key] = (player as any).param[key];\n        });\n        return {\n          [ATK]: (player as any).atk,\n          [PDEF]: (player as any).pdef,\n          [SDEF]: (player as any).sdef,\n          ...params,\n        };\n      };\n      let damage = 0,\n        fn;\n      let critical = false;\n      let guard = false;\n      let superGuard = false;\n      let elementVulnerable = false;\n      const paramA = getParam(attackerPlayer);\n      const paramB = getParam(self as any);\n      if (skill) {\n        fn = self.getFormulas(\"damageSkill\");\n        if (!fn) {\n          throw new Error(\"Skill Formulas not exists\");\n        }\n        damage = fn(paramA, paramB, skill);\n      } else {\n        fn = self.getFormulas(\"damagePhysic\");\n        if (!fn) {\n          throw new Error(\"Physic Formulas not exists\");\n        }\n        damage = fn(paramA, paramB);\n        const coef = self.coefficientElements(attackerPlayer);\n        if (coef >= 2) {\n          elementVulnerable = true;\n        }\n        damage *= coef;\n        fn = self.getFormulas(\"damageCritical\");\n        if (fn) {\n          let newDamage = fn(damage, paramA, paramB);\n          if (damage != newDamage) {\n            critical = true;\n          }\n          damage = newDamage;\n        }\n      }\n      if (self.hasEffect(Effect.GUARD)) {\n        fn = self.getFormulas(\"damageGuard\");\n        if (fn) {\n          let newDamage = fn(damage, paramA, paramB);\n          if (damage != newDamage) {\n            guard = true;\n          }\n          damage = newDamage;\n        }\n      }\n      if (self.hasEffect(Effect.SUPER_GUARD)) {\n        damage /= 4;\n        superGuard = true;\n      }\n      self.hp -= damage;\n      return {\n        damage,\n        critical,\n        elementVulnerable,\n        guard,\n        superGuard,\n      };\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get damage formulas from the current map\n     * \n     * Retrieves the damage calculation formulas defined in the current map's configuration.\n     * These formulas are used to calculate different types of damage including physical,\n     * magical, critical hits, and guard effects. The formulas provide flexibility in\n     * customizing the battle system's damage calculations.\n     * \n     * @param name - The name of the formula to retrieve (e.g., 'damagePhysic', 'damageSkill')\n     * @returns The formula function or undefined if not found\n     * \n     * @example\n     * ```ts\n     * // Get physical damage formula\n     * const physicFormula = player.getFormulas('damagePhysic');\n     * if (physicFormula) {\n     *   const damage = physicFormula(attackerParams, defenderParams);\n     * }\n     * \n     * // Get critical damage formula\n     * const criticalFormula = player.getFormulas('damageCritical');\n     * if (criticalFormula) {\n     *   const criticalDamage = criticalFormula(baseDamage, attackerParams, defenderParams);\n     * }\n     * ```\n     */\n    getFormulas(name: string) {\n      const self = this as unknown as PlayerWithMixins;\n      const map = self.getCurrentMap(); \n      return map?.damageFormulas[name];\n    }\n  } as unknown as any;\n}","import { Constructor, isString, PlayerCtor, RpgCommonPlayer } from \"@rpgjs/common\";\n\ntype ClassClass = any;\ntype ActorClass = any;\n\ninterface PlayerWithMixins extends RpgCommonPlayer {\n  databaseById(id: string): any;\n  addParameter(name: string, { start, end }: { start: number, end: number }): void;\n  addItem(item: any): any;\n  equip(itemId: string, equip?: boolean | 'auto'): void;\n}\n\n/**\n * Class Manager Mixin\n * \n * Provides class and actor management capabilities to any class. This mixin handles\n * character class assignment and actor setup, including automatic parameter configuration,\n * starting equipment, and skill progression based on class definitions.\n * \n * @param Base - The base class to extend with class management\n * @returns Extended class with class management methods\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * class MyPlayer extends WithClassManager(BasePlayer) {\n *   constructor() {\n *     super();\n *     // Class system is automatically initialized\n *   }\n * }\n * \n * const player = new MyPlayer();\n * player.setClass(Fighter);\n * player.setActor(Hero);\n * ```\n */\nexport function WithClassManager<TBase extends PlayerCtor>(Base: TBase) {\n  return class extends Base {\n    private _resolveClassInput(classInput: ClassClass | string, databaseByIdOverride?: (id: string) => any) {\n      if (isString(classInput)) {\n        return databaseByIdOverride\n          ? databaseByIdOverride(classInput)\n          : (this as any).databaseById(classInput);\n      }\n      return classInput;\n    }\n\n    private _createClassInstance(classInput: ClassClass | string) {\n      const classClass = this._resolveClassInput(classInput);\n      const instance = new (classClass as ClassClass)();\n      return { classClass, instance };\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Create a class instance without side effects.\n     */\n    createClassInstance(classInput: ClassClass | string) {\n      return this._createClassInstance(classInput);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Resolve class snapshot entry into a class instance without side effects.\n     */\n    resolveClassSnapshot(snapshot: { _class?: any }, mapOverride?: any) {\n      if (!snapshot || snapshot._class == null) {\n        return snapshot;\n      }\n\n      const map = mapOverride ?? ((this as any).getCurrentMap?.() || (this as any).map);\n      if (!map || !map.database) {\n        return snapshot;\n      }\n\n      const databaseByIdOverride = (id: string) => {\n        const data = map.database()[id];\n        if (!data) {\n          throw new Error(\n            `The ID=${id} data is not found in the database. Add the data in the property \"database\"`\n          );\n        }\n        return data;\n      };\n\n      const classId = isString(snapshot._class) ? snapshot._class : snapshot._class?.id;\n      if (!classId) {\n        return snapshot;\n      }\n\n      const classClass = this._resolveClassInput(classId, databaseByIdOverride);\n      const { instance } = this._createClassInstance(classClass as ClassClass);\n      return { ...snapshot, _class: instance };\n    }\n\n    setClass(_class: ClassClass | string) {\n      const { instance } = this._createClassInstance(_class);\n      const classInstance = instance;\n      (this as any)[\"execMethod\"](\"onSet\", [this], classInstance);\n      return classInstance;\n    }\n\n    setActor(actorClass: ActorClass | string) {\n      if (isString(actorClass)) actorClass = (this as any).databaseById(actorClass);\n      const actor = new (actorClass as ActorClass)();\n      [\"name\", \"initialLevel\", \"finalLevel\", \"expCurve\"].forEach((key) => {\n        if (actor[key]) (this as any)[key] = actor[key];\n      });\n      for (let param in actor.parameters) {\n        (this as any).addParameter(param, actor.parameters[param]);\n      }\n      for (let item of actor.startingEquipment) {\n        const inventory = (this as any).addItem(item);\n        const itemId = inventory?.id?.();\n        if (itemId) {\n          (this as any).equip(itemId, true);\n        }\n      }\n      if (actor.class) this.setClass(actor.class);\n      (this as any)[\"execMethod\"](\"onSet\", [this], actor);\n      return actor;\n    }\n  } as unknown as TBase;\n}\n\n/**\n * Interface for Class Manager functionality\n * \n * Provides class and actor management capabilities including character class assignment\n * and actor setup. This mixin handles\n * player states (buffs/debuffs), state defense from equipment, and state\n * efficiency modifiers. It manages the complete state system including\n * application, removal, and resistance mechanics.\n * \n * @param Base - The base class to extend with state management\n * @returns Extended class with state management methods\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * class MyPlayer extends WithStateManager(BasePlayer) {\n *   constructor() {\n *     super();\n *     // State system is automatically initialized\n *   }\n * }\n * \n * const player = new MyPlayer();\n * player.addState(Paralyze);\n * console.log(player.getState(Paralyze));\n * ```\n */\nexport function WithStateManager<TBase extends PlayerCtor>(Base: TBase) {\n  return class extends Base {\n    _statesEfficiency = signal<any[]>([]);\n\n    private _getStateMap(required: boolean = true) {\n      // Use this.map directly to support both RpgMap and LobbyRoom\n      const map = (this as any).getCurrentMap?.() || (this as any).map;\n      if (required && (!map || !map.database)) {\n        throw new Error('Player must be on a map to resolve states');\n      }\n      return map;\n    }\n\n    private _resolveStateInput(\n      stateInput: StateClass | string,\n      databaseByIdOverride?: (id: string) => any\n    ) {\n      if (isString(stateInput)) {\n        return databaseByIdOverride\n          ? this._getStateMap(false);\n      if (!map || !map.database) {\n        return snapshot;\n      }\n\n      const databaseByIdOverride = (id: string) => {\n        const data = map.database()[id];\n        if (!data) {\n          throw new Error(\n            `The ID=${id} data is not found in the database. Add the data in the property \"database\"`\n          );\n        }\n        return data;\n      };\n\n      const states = snapshot.states.map((entry: any) => {\n        const stateId = isString(entry) ? entry : entry?.id;\n        if (!stateId) {\n          return entry;\n        }\n        const stateClass = this._resolveStateInput(stateId, databaseByIdOverride);\n        return this._createStateInstance(stateClass as StateClass);\n      });\n\n      return { ...snapshot, states };\n    }\n\n    get statesDefense(): { rate: number; state: any }[] {\n      return (this as any).getFeature(\"statesDefense\", \"state\");\n    }\n\n    get statesEfficiency() {\n      return this._statesEfficiency;\n    }\n\n    set statesEfficiency(val) {\n      this._statesEfficiency = val;\n    }\n\n    applyStates(\n      player: RpgPlayer,\n      { addStates, removeStates }\n    ) {\n      if (addStates) {\n        for (let { state, rate } of addStates) {\n          (player as any).addState(state, rate);\n        }\n      }\n      if (removeStates) {\n        for (let { state, rate } of removeStates) {\n          (player as any).removeState(state, rate);\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    getState(stateClass: StateClass | string) {\n      if (isString(stateClass)) stateClass = (this as any).databaseById(stateClass);\n      return this.states().find((state) => {\n        if (isString(stateClass)) {\n          return state.id == stateClass;\n        }\n        return isInstanceOf(state, stateClass);\n      });\n    }\n\n    addState(stateClass: StateClass | string, chance = 1): object | null {\n      const state = this.getState(stateClass);\n      if (isString(stateClass)) {\n        stateClass = (this as any).databaseById(stateClass);\n      }\n      if (!state) {\n        if (Math.random() > chance) {\n          throw StateLog.addFailed(stateClass);\n        }\n        //const efficiency = this.findStateEfficiency(stateClass)\n        const instance = this._createStateInstance(stateClass as StateClass);\n        this.states().push(instance);\n        this.applyStates(<any>this, instance);\n        return instance;\n      }\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    removeState(stateClass: StateClass | string, chance = 1) {\n      const index = this.states().findIndex((state) => {\n        if (isString(stateClass)) {\n          return state.id == stateClass;\n        }\n        return isInstanceOf(state, stateClass);\n      });\n      if (index != -1) {\n        if (Math.random() > chance) {\n          throw StateLog.removeFailed(stateClass);\n        }\n        this.states().splice(index, 1);\n      } else {\n        throw StateLog.notApplied(stateClass);\n      }\n    }\n\n    findStateEfficiency(stateClass) {\n      return this.statesEfficiency().find((state) =>\n        isInstanceOf(state.state, stateClass)\n      );\n    }\n  } as unknown as TBase;\n}\n\n/**\n * loops\n  private _clientListeners = new Map<string, Set<(data: any) => void | Promise<void>>>();\n\n  /**\n   * Computed signal for world X position\n   * \n   * Calculates the absolute world X position from the map's world position\n   * plus the player's local X position. Returns 0 if no map is assigned.\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * const worldX = player.worldX();\n   * console.log(`Player is at world X: ${worldX}`);\n   * ```\n   */\n  get worldPositionX() {\n    return this._getComputedWorldPosition('x');\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Computed signal for world Y position\n   * \n   * Calculates the absolute world Y position from the map's world position\n   * plus the player's local Y position. Returns 0 if no map is assigned.\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * const worldY = player.worldY();\n   * console.log(`Player is at world Y: ${worldY}`);\n   * ```\n   */\n  get worldPositionY() {\n    return this._getComputedWorldPosition('y');\n  }\n\n  private _worldPositionSignals = new WeakMap<any, any>();\n\n  private _getComputedWorldPosition(axis: 'x' | 'y') {\n    // We use a WeakMap to cache the computed signal per instance\n    // This ensures that if the player object is copied (e.g. in tests),\n    // the new instance gets its own signal bound to itself.\n    if (!this._worldPositionSignals) {\n      this._worldPositionSignals = new WeakMap();\n    }\n\n    const key = axis;\n    let signals = this._worldPositionSignals.get(this);\n    if (!signals) {\n      signals = {};\n      this._worldPositionSignals.set(this, signals);\n    }\n\n    if (!signals[key]) {\n      signals[key] = computed(() => {\n        const map = this.map as RpgMap | null;\n        const mapWorldPos = map ? (map[axis === 'x' ? 'worldX' : 'worldY'] ?? 0) : 0;\n        return mapWorldPos + (this[axis] as any)();\n      });\n    }\n    return signals[key];\n  }\n\n  /** Internal: Shapes attached to this player */\n  private _attachedShapes: Map<string, RpgShape> = new Map();\n\n  /** Internal: Shapes where this player is currently located */\n  private _inShapes: Set<RpgShape> = new Set();\n  /** Last processed client input timestamp for reconciliation */\n  lastProcessedInputTs: number = 0;\n  /** Last processed client input frame for reconciliation with server tick */\n  _lastFramePositions: {\n    frame: number;\n    position: {\n      x: number;\n      y: number;\n      direction: Direction;\n    };\n    serverTick?: number; // Server tick at which this position was computed\n  } | null = null;\n\n  frames: { x: number; y: number; ts: number }[] = [];\n\n  @sync(RpgPlayer) events = signal<RpgEvent[]>([]);\n\n  constructor() {\n    super();\n\n    const initialX = typeof this.x === \"function\" ? Number(this.x()) || 0 : 0;\n    const initialY = typeof this.y === \"function\" ? Number(this.y()) || 0 : 0;\n    let lastEmitted: { x: number; y: number } | null = { x: initialX, y: initialY };\n    let pendingUpdate: { x: number; y: number } | null = null;\n    let updateScheduled = false;\n\n    combineLatest([this.x.observable, this.y.observable])\n      .subscribe(([x, y]) => {\n        pendingUpdate = { x, y };\n\n        // Schedule a synchronous update using queueMicrotask\n        // This groups multiple rapid changes (x and y in the same tick) into a single frame\n        if (!updateScheduled) {\n          updateScheduled = true;\n          queueMicrotask(() => {\n            if (pendingUpdate) {\n              const { x, y } = pendingUpdate;\n              // Only emit if the values are different from the last emitted frame\n              if (!lastEmitted || lastEmitted.x !== x || lastEmitted.y !== y) {\n                this.frames = [...this.frames, {\n                  x: x,\n                  y: y,\n                  ts: Date.now(),\n                }];\n                lastEmitted = { x, y };\n              }\n              pendingUpdate = null;\n            }\n            updateScheduled = false;\n          });\n        }\n      })\n  }\n\n  private _getClientListenerBucket(key: string) {\n    let listeners = this._clientListeners.get(key);\n    if (!listeners) {\n      listeners = new Set();\n      this._clientListeners.set(key, listeners);\n    }\n    return listeners;\n  }\n\n  async _dispatchClientEvent(key: string, data: any) {\n    const listeners = [...(this._clientListeners.get(key) ?? [])];\n    for (const callback of listeners) {\n      await callback(data);\n    }\n  }\n\n  _onInit() {\n    this.hooks.callHooks(\"server-playerProps-load\", this).subscribe();\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Apply the built-in default parameter curves to this player.\n   *\n   * Use this when you want RPGJS to provide the initial parameter setup\n   * instead of restoring values from your own database or a saved snapshot.\n   *\n   * This method only defines the parameter curves and related defaults.\n   * It does not restore custom persisted data for you.\n   *\n   * @method player.applyDefaultParameters()\n   * @returns {void}\n   */\n  applyDefaultParameters() {\n    // Use type assertion to access mixin properties\n    (this as any).expCurve = {\n      basis: 30,\n      extra: 20,\n      accelerationA: 30,\n      accelerationB: 30\n    };\n\n    ;(this as any).addParameter(MAXHP, MAXHP_CURVE);\n    (this as any).addParameter(MAXSP, MAXSP_CURVE);\n    (this as any).addParameter(STR, STR_CURVE);\n    (this as any).addParameter(INT, INT_CURVE);\n    (this as any).addParameter(DEX, DEX_CURVE);\n    (this as any).addParameter(AGI, AGI_CURVE);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Initialize the built-in default player stats.\n   *\n   * This applies the default parameter curves and then restores HP/SP to their\n   * current maximum values so the client receives coherent bars on first load.\n   *\n   * Call this manually in `onConnected()` or `onStart()` when your game relies\n   * on the built-in defaults. Do not call it after loading a snapshot or\n   * hydrating player data from your own database unless you explicitly want to\n   * overwrite those values.\n   *\n   * @method player.initializeDefaultStats()\n   * @returns {void}\n   */\n  initializeDefaultStats() {\n    this.applyDefaultParameters();\n    (this as any).allRecovery();\n  }\n\n  get hooks() {\n    return inject<Hooks>(this.context as any, ModulesToken);\n  }\n\n  // compatibility with v4\n  get server() {\n    return this.map\n  }\n\n  setMap(map: RpgMap) {\n    this.map = map;\n    // Prevent immediate ping-pong map transfers when spawning near a border.\n    this.touchSide = true;\n  }\n\n  applyFrames() {\n    this._frames.set(this.frames)\n    this.frames = []\n  }\n\n  async execMethod(method: string, methodData: any[] = [], target?: any) {\n    let ret: any;\n    if (target) {\n      if (typeof target[method] === 'function') {\n        ret = await target[method](...methodData);\n      }\n    }\n    else {\n      ret = await lastValueFrom(this.hooks\n        .callHooks(`server-player-${method}`, target ?? this, ...methodData));\n    }\n    this.syncChanges()\n    return ret;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Change the map for this player\n   *\n   * @param mapId - The ID of the map to change to\n   * @param positions - Optional positions to place the player at\n   * @returns A promise that resolves when the map change is complete\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Change player to map \"town\" at position {x: 10, y: 20}\n   * await player.changeMap(\"town\", {x: 10, y: 20});\n   *\n   * // Change player to map \"dungeon\" at a named position\n   * await player.changeMap(\"dungeon\", \"entrance\");\n   * ```\n   */\n  async changeMap(\n    mapId: string,\n    positions?: { x: number; y: number; z?: number } | string\n  ): Promise<any | null | boolean> {\n    const realMapId = 'map-' + mapId;\n    const room = this.getCurrentMap();\n\n    const canChange: boolean[] = await lastValueFrom(this.hooks.callHooks(\"server-player-canChangeMap\", this, {\n      id: mapId,\n    }));\n    if (canChange.some(v => v === false)) return false;\n\n    if (positions && typeof positions === 'object') {\n      await this.teleport(positions)\n    }\n    const transferToken = await room?.$sessionTransfer(this.conn, realMapId);\n    this.emit(\"changeMap\", {\n      mapId: realMapId,\n      positions,\n      transferToken: typeof transferToken === 'string' ? transferToken : undefined,\n    });\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  async autoChangeMap(nextPosition: Vector2): Promise<boolean> {\n    const map = this.getCurrentMap()\n    const worldMaps = map?.getInWorldMaps()\n    let ret: boolean = false\n    if (worldMaps && map) {\n        const direction = this.getDirection()\n        const marginLeftRight = map.tileWidth / 2\n        const marginTopDown = map.tileHeight / 2\n        const hitbox = this.hitbox()\n        const currentX = this.x()\n        const currentY = this.y()\n        const nearBorder =\n          currentX < marginLeftRight ||\n          currentX > map.widthPx - hitbox.w - marginLeftRight ||\n          currentY < marginTopDown ||\n          currentY > map.heightPx - hitbox.h - marginTopDown\n\n        if (this.touchSide) {\n            if (nearBorder) {\n                return false\n            }\n            this.touchSide = false\n        }\n\n        const changeMap = async (adjacent, to) => {\n            const [nextMap] = worldMaps.getAdjacentMaps(map, adjacent)\n            if (!nextMap) {\n                return false\n            }\n            const id = nextMap.id as string\n            const nextMapInfo = worldMaps.getMapInfo(id)\n            const changed = !!(await this.changeMap(id, to(nextMapInfo)))\n            if (changed) {\n                this.touchSide = true\n            }\n            return changed\n        }\n\n        if (nextPosition.x < marginLeftRight && direction == Direction.Left) {\n            ret = await changeMap({\n                x: map.worldX - 1,\n                y: this.worldPositionY() + 1\n            }, nextMapInfo => ({\n                x: (nextMapInfo.width) - this.hitbox().w - marginLeftRight,\n                y: map.worldY - nextMapInfo.y + nextPosition.y\n            }))\n        }\n        else if (nextPosition.x > map.widthPx - this.hitbox().w - marginLeftRight && direction == Direction.Right) {\n            ret = await changeMap({\n                x: map.worldX + map.widthPx + 1,\n                y: this.worldPositionY() + 1\n            }, nextMapInfo => ({\n                x: marginLeftRight,\n                y: map.worldY - nextMapInfo.y + nextPosition.y\n            }))\n        }\n        else if (nextPosition.y < marginTopDown && direction == Direction.Up) {\n            ret = await changeMap({\n                x: this.worldPositionX() + 1,\n                y: map.worldY - 1\n            }, nextMapInfo => ({\n                x: map.worldX - nextMapInfo.x + nextPosition.x,\n                y: (nextMapInfo.height) - this.hitbox().h - marginTopDown,\n            }))\n        }\n        else if (nextPosition.y > map.heightPx - this.hitbox().h - marginTopDown && direction == Direction.Down) {\n            ret = await changeMap({\n                x: this.worldPositionX() + 1,\n                y: map.worldY + map.heightPx + 1\n            }, nextMapInfo => ({\n                x: map.worldX - nextMapInfo.x + nextPosition.x,\n                y: marginTopDown,\n            }))\n        }\n        else {\n            this.touchSide = false\n        }\n    }\n    return ret\n}\n\n  async teleport(positions: { x: number; y: number }) {\n    if (!this.map) return false;\n    if (this.map && this.map.physic) {\n      // Skip collision check for teleportation (allow teleporting through walls)\n      const entity = this.map.physic.getEntityByUUID(this.id);\n      if (entity) {\n        const hitbox = typeof this.hitbox === \"function\" ? this.hitbox() : this.hitbox;\n        const width = hitbox?.w ?? 32;\n        const height = hitbox?.h ?? 32;\n        \n        // Convert top-left position to center position for physics engine\n        // positions.x/y are TOP-LEFT coordinates, but physic.teleport expects CENTER coordinates\n        const centerX = positions.x + width / 2;\n        const centerY = positions.y + height / 2;\n        \n        this.map.physic.teleport(entity, { x: centerX, y: centerY });\n      }\n    }\n    this.x.set(positions.x)\n    this.y.set(positions.y)\n    // Wait for the frame to be added before applying frames\n    // This ensures the frame is added before applyFrames() is called\n    queueMicrotask(() => {\n      this.applyFrames()\n    })\n  }\n\n  getCurrentMap<T extends RpgMap = RpgMap>(): T | null {\n    return this.map as T | null;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Send a custom event to the current player's client.\n   *\n   * Use this to push arbitrary websocket payloads to one client only.\n   * On the client side, receive the event by injecting `WebSocketToken`\n   * and subscribing with `socket.on(...)`.\n   *\n   * @method player.emit(type, value)\n   * @param type - Custom event name sent to the client\n   * @param value - Payload sent with the event\n   * @returns {void}\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * player.emit(\"inventory:updated\", {\n   *   slots: player.items().length,\n   * });\n   * ```\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * import { inject } from \"@rpgjs/client\";\n   * import { WebSocketToken, type AbstractWebsocket } from \"@rpgjs/client\";\n   *\n   * const socket = inject<AbstractWebsocket>(WebSocketToken);\n   *\n   * socket.on(\"inventory:updated\", (payload) => {\n   *   console.log(payload.slots);\n   * });\n   * ```\n   */\n  emit(type: string, value?: any) {\n    const map = this.getCurrentMap();\n    if (!map || !this.conn) return;\n    map.$send(this.conn, {\n      type,\n      value,\n    });\n  }\n\n  snapshot() {\n    const snapshot = createStatesSnapshotDeep(this);\n    const expCurve = (this as any).expCurve;\n    if (expCurve) {\n      snapshot.expCurve = { ...expCurve };\n    }\n    return snapshot;\n  }\n\n  async applySnapshot(snapshot: string | object) {\n    const data = typeof snapshot === \"string\" ? JSON.parse(snapshot) : snapshot;\n    const withItems = (this as any).resolveItemsSnapshot?.(data) ?? data;\n    const withSkills = (this as any).resolveSkillsSnapshot?.(withItems) ?? withItems;\n    const withStates = (this as any).resolveStatesSnapshot?.(withSkills) ?? withSkills;\n    const withClass = (this as any).resolveClassSnapshot?.(withStates) ?? withStates;\n    const resolvedSnapshot = (this as any).resolveEquipmentsSnapshot?.(withClass) ?? withClass;\n    load(this, resolvedSnapshot);\n    if (resolvedSnapshot.expCurve) {\n      (this as any).expCurve = resolvedSnapshot.expCurve;\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(resolvedSnapshot.items)) {\n      this.items.set(resolvedSnapshot.items);\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(resolvedSnapshot.skills)) {\n      this.skills.set(resolvedSnapshot.skills);\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(resolvedSnapshot.states)) {\n      this.states.set(resolvedSnapshot.states);\n    }\n    if (resolvedSnapshot._class != null && this._class?.set) {\n      this._class.set(resolvedSnapshot._class);\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(resolvedSnapshot.equipments)) {\n      this.equipments.set(resolvedSnapshot.equipments);\n    }\n    await lastValueFrom(this.hooks.callHooks(\"server-player-onLoad\", this, resolvedSnapshot));\n    return resolvedSnapshot;\n  }\n\n  async save(slot: SaveSlotIndex = \"auto\", meta: SaveSlotMeta = {}, context: SaveRequestContext = {}) {\n    const policy = resolveAutoSaveStrategy();\n    if (policy.canSave && !policy.canSave(this, context)) {\n      return null;\n    }\n    const resolvedSlot = resolveSaveSlot(slot, policy, this, context);\n    if (resolvedSlot === null) {\n      return null;\n    }\n    const snapshot = this.snapshot();\n    await lastValueFrom(this.hooks.callHooks(\"server-player-onSave\", this, snapshot));\n    const storage = resolveSaveStorageStrategy();\n    const finalMeta = buildSaveSlotMeta(this, meta);\n    await storage.save(this, resolvedSlot, JSON.stringify(snapshot), finalMeta);\n    return { index: resolvedSlot, meta: finalMeta };\n  }\n\n  async load(\n    slot: SaveSlotIndex = \"auto\",\n    context: SaveRequestContext = {},\n    options: { changeMap?: boolean } = {}\n  ) {\n    const policy = resolveAutoSaveStrategy();\n    if (policy.canLoad && !policy.canLoad(this, context)) {\n      return { ok: false };\n    }\n    const resolvedSlot = resolveSaveSlot(slot, policy, this, context);\n    if (resolvedSlot === null) {\n      return { ok: false };\n    }\n    const storage = resolveSaveStorageStrategy();\n    const slotData = await storage.get(this, resolvedSlot);\n    if (!slotData?.snapshot) {\n      return { ok: false };\n    }\n    await this.applySnapshot(slotData.snapshot);\n    const { snapshot, ...meta } = slotData;\n    if (options.changeMap !== false && meta.map) {\n      await this.changeMap(meta.map);\n    }\n    return { ok: true, slot: meta, index: resolvedSlot };\n  }\n\n \n  /**\n   * @deprecated Use setGraphicAnimation instead.\n   * @param animationName - The name of the animation to play (e.g., 'attack', 'skill', 'walk')\n   * @param nbTimes - Number of times to repeat the animation (default: Infinity for continuous)\n   */\n  setAnimation(animationName: string, nbTimes: number = Infinity) {\n    console.warn('setAnimation is deprecated. Use setGraphicAnimation instead.');\n    this.setGraphicAnimation(animationName, nbTimes);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * @deprecated Use setGraphicAnimation instead.\n   * @param graphic - The graphic to use for the animation (e.g., 'attack', 'skill', 'walk')\n   * @param animationName - The name of the animation to play (e.g., 'attack', 'skill', 'walk')\n   * @param replaceGraphic - Whether to replace the player's graphic (default: false)\n   */\n  showAnimation(graphic: string, animationName: string, replaceGraphic: boolean = false) {\n    if (replaceGraphic) {\n      console.warn('showAnimation is deprecated. Use player.setGraphicAnimation instead.');\n      this.setGraphicAnimation(animationName, graphic);\n    }\n    else {\n      console.warn('showAnimation is deprecated. Use map.showAnimation instead.');\n      const map = this.getCurrentMap();\n      map?.showAnimation({ x: this.x(), y: this.y() }, graphic, animationName);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Listen to custom data sent by the current player's client.\n   *\n   * This listens to websocket actions emitted from the client with\n   * `socket.emit(key, data)`. It is intended for custom client events\n   * that are not already handled by built-in server actions such as\n   * `move`, `action`, or GUI interactions.\n   *\n   * @title Listen to data from the client\n   * @method player.on(key, cb)\n   * @param key - Event name emitted by the client\n   * @param cb - Callback invoked with the payload sent by the client\n   * @returns {void}\n   * @since 3.0.0-beta.5\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * player.on(\"chat:message\", ({ text }) => {\n   *   console.log(\"Client says:\", text);\n   * });\n   * ```\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * import { inject } from \"@rpgjs/client\";\n   * import { WebSocketToken, type AbstractWebsocket } from \"@rpgjs/client\";\n   *\n   * const socket = inject<AbstractWebsocket>(WebSocketToken);\n   * socket.emit(\"chat:message\", { text: \"Hello server\" });\n   * ```\n   */\n  on(key: string, cb: (data: any) => void | Promise<void>) {\n    this._getClientListenerBucket(key).add(cb);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Listen one time to custom data sent by the current player's client.\n   *\n   * After the first matching event is received, the listener is removed\n   * automatically.\n   *\n   * @title Listen one-time to data from the client\n   * @method player.once(key, cb)\n   * @param key - Event name emitted by the client\n   * @param cb - Callback invoked only once with the payload sent by the client\n   * @returns {void}\n   * @since 3.0.0-beta.5\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * player.once(\"tutorial:ready\", (payload) => {\n   *   console.log(\"Ready once:\", payload.step);\n   * });\n   * ```\n   */\n  once(key: string, cb: (data: any) => void | Promise<void>) {\n    const onceCallback = async (data: any) => {\n      this._clientListeners.get(key)?.delete(onceCallback);\n      await cb(data);\n    };\n    this.on(key, onceCallback);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Remove all listeners for a custom client event on this player.\n   *\n   * @title Remove listeners of the client event\n   * @method player.off(key)\n   * @param key - Event name to clear\n   * @returns {void}\n   * @since 3.0.0-beta.5\n   *\n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * player.off(\"chat:message\");\n   * ```\n   */\n  off(key: string) {\n    this._clientListeners.delete(key);\n  }\n\n   /**\n   * Set the current animation of the player's sprite\n   *\n   * This method changes the animation state of the player's current sprite.\n   * It's used to trigger character animations like attack, skill, or custom movements.\n   * When `nbTimes` is set to a finite number, the animation will play that many times\n   * before returning to the previous animation state.\n   *\n   * If `animationFixed` is true, this method will not change the animation.\n   *\n   * @param animationName - The name of the animation to play (e.g., 'attack', 'skill', 'walk')\n   * @param nbTimes - Number of times to repeat the animation (default: Infinity for continuous)\n   */\n  setGraphicAnimation(animationName: string, nbTimes: number): void;\n  /**\n   * Set the current animation of the player's sprite with a temporary graphic change\n   *\n   * This method changes the animation state of the player's current sprite and temporarily\n   * changes the player's graphic (sprite sheet) during the animation. The graphic is\n   * automatically reset when the animation finishes.\n   *\n   * When `nbTimes` is set to a finite number, the animation will play that many times\n   * before returning to the previous animation state and graphic.\n   *\n   * If `animationFixed` is true, this method will not change the animation.\n   *\n   * @param animationName - The name of the animation to play (e.g., 'attack', 'skill', 'walk')\n   * @param graphic - The graphic(s) to temporarily use during the animation\n   * @param nbTimes - Number of times to repeat the animation (default: Infinity for continuous)\n   */\n  setGraphicAnimation(animationName: string, graphic: string | string[], nbTimes: number): void;\n  setGraphicAnimation(animationName: string, graphic: string | string[]): void;\n  setGraphicAnimation(animationName: string, graphicOrNbTimes?: string | string[] | number, nbTimes: number = 1): void {\n    // Don't change animation if it's locked\n    if (this.animationFixed) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    let graphic: string | string[] | undefined;\n    let finalNbTimes: number = Infinity;\n\n    // Handle overloads\n    if (typeof graphicOrNbTimes === 'number') {\n      // setGraphicAnimation(animationName, nbTimes)\n      finalNbTimes = graphicOrNbTimes;\n    } else if (graphicOrNbTimes !== undefined) {\n      // setGraphicAnimation(animationName, graphic, nbTimes)\n      graphic = graphicOrNbTimes;\n      finalNbTimes = nbTimes ?? Infinity;\n    } else {\n      // setGraphicAnimation(animationName) - nbTimes remains Infinity\n      finalNbTimes = Infinity;\n    }\n\n    const map = this.getCurrentMap();\n    if (!map) return;\n\n    if (finalNbTimes === Infinity) {\n      if (graphic) this.setGraphic(graphic);\n      this.animationName.set(animationName);\n    }\n    else {\n      map.$broadcast({\n        type: \"setAnimation\",\n        value: {\n          animationName,\n          graphic,\n          nbTimes: finalNbTimes,\n          object: this.id,\n        },\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n\n  /**\n   * Run the change detection cycle. Normally, as soon as a hook is called in a class, the cycle is started. But you can start it manually\n   * The method calls the `onChanges` method on events and synchronizes all map data with the client.\n\n  * @title Run Sync Changes\n  * @method player.syncChanges()\n  * @returns {void}\n  * @memberof Player\n  */\n  syncChanges() {\n    this._eventChanges();\n    if (shouldAutoSave(this, { reason: \"auto\", source: \"syncChanges\" })) {\n      void this.save(\"auto\", {}, { reason: \"auto\", source: \"syncChanges\" });\n    }\n  }\n\n  databaseById(id: string) {\n    // Use this.map directly to support both RpgMap and LobbyRoom\n    const map = this.map as any;\n    if (!map || !map.database) return;\n    const data = map.database()[id];\n    if (!data)\n      throw new Error(\n        `The ID=${id} data is not found in the database. Add the data in the property \"database\"`\n      );\n    return data;\n  }\n\n  private _eventChanges() {\n    const map = this.getCurrentMap();\n    if (!map) return;\n    const { events } = map;\n    const visibleMapEvents = Object.values(events?.() ?? {}).filter((event: any) =>\n      map.isEventVisibleForPlayer?.(event, this) ?? true\n    );\n    const arrayEvents: any[] = [\n      ...Object.values(this.events()),\n      ...visibleMapEvents,\n    ];\n    for (let event of arrayEvents) {\n      if (event.onChanges) event.onChanges(this);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Attach a zone shape to this player using the physic zone system\n   * \n   * This method creates a zone attached to the player's entity in the physics engine.\n   * The zone can be circular or cone-shaped and will detect other entities (players/events)\n   * entering or exiting the zone.\n   * \n   * @param id - Optional zone identifier. If not provided, a unique ID will be generated\n   * @param options - Zone configuration options\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Create a circular detection zone\n   * player.attachShape(\"vision\", {\n   *   radius: 150,\n   *   angle: 360,\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Create a cone-shaped vision zone\n   * player.attachShape(\"vision\", {\n   *   radius: 200,\n   *   angle: 120,\n   *   direction: Direction.Right,\n   *   limitedByWalls: true,\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Create a zone with width/height (radius calculated automatically)\n   * player.attachShape({\n   *   width: 100,\n   *   height: 100,\n   *   positioning: \"center\",\n   * });\n   * ```\n   */\n  attachShape(idOrOptions: string | AttachShapeOptions, options?: AttachShapeOptions): RpgShape | undefined {\n    const map = this.getCurrentMap();\n    if (!map) return undefined;\n\n    // Handle overloaded signature: attachShape(options) or attachShape(id, options)\n    let zoneId: string;\n    let shapeOptions: AttachShapeOptions;\n\n    if (typeof idOrOptions === 'string') {\n      zoneId = idOrOptions;\n      if (!options) {\n        console.warn('attachShape: options must be provided when id is specified');\n        return undefined;\n      }\n      shapeOptions = options;\n    } else {\n      zoneId = `zone-${this.id}-${Date.now()}`;\n      shapeOptions = idOrOptions;\n    }\n\n    // Get player entity from physic engine\n    const playerEntity = map.physic.getEntityByUUID(this.id);\n    if (!playerEntity) {\n      console.warn(`Player entity not found in physic engine for player ${this.id}`);\n      return undefined;\n    }\n\n    // Calculate radius from width/height if not provided\n    let radius: number;\n    if (shapeOptions.radius !== undefined) {\n      radius = shapeOptions.radius;\n    } else if (shapeOptions.width && shapeOptions.height) {\n      // Use the larger dimension as radius, or calculate from area\n      radius = Math.max(shapeOptions.width, shapeOptions.height) / 2;\n    } else {\n      console.warn('attachShape: radius or width/height must be provided');\n      return undefined;\n    }\n\n    // Calculate offset based on positioning\n    let offset: Vector2 = new Vector2(0, 0);\n    const positioning: ShapePositioning = shapeOptions.positioning || \"default\";\n    if (shapeOptions.positioning) {\n      const playerWidth = playerEntity.width || playerEntity.radius * 2 || 32;\n      const playerHeight = playerEntity.height || playerEntity.radius * 2 || 32;\n\n      switch (shapeOptions.positioning) {\n        case 'top':\n          offset = new Vector2(0, -playerHeight / 2);\n          break;\n        case 'bottom':\n          offset = new Vector2(0, playerHeight / 2);\n          break;\n        case 'left':\n          offset = new Vector2(-playerWidth / 2, 0);\n          break;\n        case 'right':\n          offset = new Vector2(playerWidth / 2, 0);\n          break;\n        case 'center':\n        default:\n          offset = new Vector2(0, 0);\n          break;\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Get zone manager and create attached zone\n    const zoneManager = map.physic.getZoneManager();\n\n    // Convert direction from Direction enum to string if needed\n    // Direction enum values are already strings (\"up\", \"down\", \"left\", \"right\")\n    let direction: 'up' | 'down' | 'left' | 'right' = 'down';\n    if (shapeOptions.direction !== undefined) {\n      if (typeof shapeOptions.direction === 'string') {\n        direction = shapeOptions.direction as 'up' | 'down' | 'left' | 'right';\n      } else {\n        // Direction enum value is already a string, just cast it\n        direction = String(shapeOptions.direction) as 'up' | 'down' | 'left' | 'right';\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Create zone with metadata for name and properties\n    const metadata: Record<string, any> = {};\n    if (shapeOptions.name) {\n      metadata.name = shapeOptions.name;\n    }\n    if (shapeOptions.properties) {\n      metadata.properties = shapeOptions.properties;\n    }\n\n    // Get initial position\n    const initialX = playerEntity.position.x + offset.x;\n    const initialY = playerEntity.position.y + offset.y;\n\n    const physicZoneId = zoneManager.createAttachedZone(\n      playerEntity,\n      {\n        radius,\n        angle: shapeOptions.angle ?? 360,\n        direction,\n        limitedByWalls: shapeOptions.limitedByWalls ?? false,\n        offset,\n        metadata: Object.keys(metadata).length > 0 ? metadata : undefined,\n      },\n      {\n        onEnter: (entities: Entity[]) => {\n          entities.forEach((entity) => {\n            const event = map.getEvent<RpgEvent>(entity.uuid);\n            const player = map.getPlayer(entity.uuid);\n\n            if (event && (!map.isEventVisibleForPlayer || map.isEventVisibleForPlayer(event, this))) {\n              event.execMethod(\"onInShape\", [shape, this]);\n              // Track that this event is in the shape\n              if ((event as any)._inShapes) {\n                (event as any)._inShapes.add(shape);\n              }\n            }\n            if (player) {\n              this.execMethod(\"onDetectInShape\", [player, shape]);\n              // Track that this player is in the shape\n              if (player._inShapes) {\n                player._inShapes.add(shape);\n              }\n            }\n          });\n        },\n        onExit: (entities: Entity[]) => {\n          entities.forEach((entity) => {\n            const event = map.getEvent<RpgEvent>(entity.uuid);\n            const player = map.getPlayer(entity.uuid);\n\n            if (event && (!map.isEventVisibleForPlayer || map.isEventVisibleForPlayer(event, this))) {\n              event.execMethod(\"onOutShape\", [shape, this]);\n              // Remove from tracking\n              if ((event as any)._inShapes) {\n                (event as any)._inShapes.delete(shape);\n              }\n            }\n            if (player) {\n              this.execMethod(\"onDetectOutShape\", [player, shape]);\n              // Remove from tracking\n              if (player._inShapes) {\n                player._inShapes.delete(shape);\n              }\n            }\n          });\n        },\n      }\n    );\n\n    // Create RpgShape instance\n    const shape = new RpgShape({\n      name: shapeOptions.name || zoneId,\n      positioning,\n      width: shapeOptions.width || radius * 2,\n      height: shapeOptions.height || radius * 2,\n      x: initialX,\n      y: initialY,\n      properties: shapeOptions.properties || {},\n      playerOwner: this,\n      physicZoneId: physicZoneId,\n      map: map,\n    });\n\n    // Store mapping from zoneId to physicZoneId for future reference\n    (this as any)._zoneIdMap = (this as any)._zoneIdMap || new Map();\n    (this as any)._zoneIdMap.set(zoneId, physicZoneId);\n\n    // Store the shape\n    this._attachedShapes.set(zoneId, shape);\n\n    // Update shape position when player moves\n    const updateShapePosition = () => {\n      const currentEntity = map.physic.getEntityByUUID(this.id);\n      if (currentEntity) {\n        const zoneInfo = zoneManager.getZone(physicZoneId);\n        if (zoneInfo) {\n          shape._updatePosition(zoneInfo.position.x, zoneInfo.position.y);\n        }\n      }\n    };\n\n    // Listen to position changes to update shape position\n    playerEntity.onPositionChange(() => {\n      updateShapePosition();\n    });\n\n    return shape;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get all shapes attached to this player\n   * \n   * Returns all shapes that were created using `attachShape()` on this sound on the client side for this player only\n   * \n   * This method emits an event to play a sound only for this specific player.\n   * The sound must be defined on the client side (in the client module configuration).\n   * \n   * ## Design\n   * \n   * The sound is sent only to this player's client connection, making it ideal\n   * for personal feedback sounds like UI interactions, The player or event that the camera should follow\n   * @param options - Camera follow options\n   * @param options.smoothMove - Enable smooth animation. Can be a boolean (default: true) or an object with animation parameters\n   * @param options.smoothMove.time - Time duration for the animation in milliseconds (optional)\n   * @param options.smoothMove.ease - Easing function name. Visit https://easings.net for available functions (optional)\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Follow another player with default smooth animation\n   * player.cameraFollow(otherPlayer, { smoothMove: true });\n   * \n   * // Follow an event with custom smooth animation\n   * player.cameraFollow(npcEvent, {\n   *   smoothMove: {\n   *     time: 1000,\n   *     ease: \"easeInOutQuad\"\n   *   }\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Follow without animation (instant)\n   * player.cameraFollow(targetPlayer, { smoothMove: false });\n   * ```\n   */\n  cameraFollow(\n    otherPlayer: RpgPlayer | RpgEvent,\n    options?: {\n      smoothMove?: boolean | { time?: number; ease?: string };\n    }\n  ): void {\n    const map = this.getCurrentMap();\n    if (!map) return;\n\n    const data: any = {\n      targetId: otherPlayer.id,\n    };\n\n    // Handle smoothMove option\n    if (options?.smoothMove !== undefined) {\n      if (typeof options.smoothMove === \"boolean\") {\n        data.smoothMove = options.smoothMove;\n      } else {\n        // smoothMove is an object\n        data.smoothMove = {\n          enabled: true,\n          ...options.smoothMove,\n        };\n      }\n    } else {\n      // Default to true if not specified\n      data.smoothMove = true;\n    }\n\n    // Send camera follow instruction only to this player\n    this.emit(\"cameraFollow\", data);\n  }\n\n\n  /**\n   * Trigger a flash animation on this player\n   * \n   * This method sends a flash animation event to the client, creating a visual\n   * feedback effect on the player's sprite. (a === b) {\n        return { valid: true, data: a };\n    }\n    if (a instanceof Date && b instanceof Date && +a === +b) {\n        return { valid: true, data: a };\n    }\n    if
