 console.warn(`Event ${id} already exists on map`);\n      return undefined;\n    }\n\n    // Check if event is a constructor function (class)\n    if (typeof event === 'function') {\n      eventInstance = new event();\n      if (event.prototype.name) eventInstance.name.set(event.prototype.name);\n    }\n    // Handle event as an object with hooks\n    else {\n      // Create a new instance extending RpgPlayer with the hooks from the event object\n      class DynamicEvent extends RpgEvent {\n        onInit?: (this: RpgEvent) => void;\n        onChanges?: (this: RpgEvent, player: RpgPlayer) => void;\n        onAction?: (this: RpgEvent, player: RpgPlayer) => void;\n        onPlayerTouch?: (this: RpgEvent, player: RpgPlayer) => void;\n        onInShape?: (this: RpgEvent, zone: RpgShape, player: RpgPlayer) => void;\n        onOutShape?: (this: RpgEvent, zone: RpgShape, player: RpgPlayer) => void;\n        onDetectInShape?: (this: RpgEvent, player: RpgPlayer, shape: RpgShape) => void;\n        onDetectOutShape?: (this: RpgEvent, player: RpgPlayer, shape: RpgShape) => void;\n\n        constructor() {\n          super();\n\n          // Copy hooks from the event object\n          const hookObj = event as EventHooks;\n          if (hookObj.onInit) this.onInit = hookObj.onInit.bind(this);\n          if (hookObj.onChanges) this.onChanges = hookObj.onChanges.bind(this);\n          if (hookObj.onAction) this.onAction = hookObj.onAction.bind(this);\n          if (hookObj.onPlayerTouch) this.onPlayerTouch = hookObj.onPlayerTouch.bind(this);\n          if (hookObj.onInShape) this.onInShape = hookObj.onInShape.bind(this);\n          if (hookObj.onOutShape) this.onOutShape = hookObj.onOutShape.bind(this);\n          if (hookObj.onDetectInShape) this.onDetectInShape = hookObj.onDetectInShape.bind(this);\n          if (hookObj.onDetectOutShape) this.onDetectOutShape = hookObj.onDetectOutShape.bind(this);\n        }\n      }\n\n      eventInstance = new DynamicEvent();\n      if ((event as any).name) eventInstance.name.set((event as any).name);\n    }\n\n    eventInstance.id = id;\n    (eventInstance as any).mode = effectiveMode;\n    if (effectiveMode === EventMode.Scenario && scenarioOwnerId) {\n      (eventInstance as any)._scenarioOwnerId = scenarioOwnerId;\n      (eventInstance as any).scenarioOwnerId = scenarioOwnerId;\n    }\n    else {\n      delete (eventInstance as any)._scenarioOwnerId;\n      delete (eventInstance as any).scenarioOwnerId;\n    }\n\n    eventInstance.map = this;\n    eventInstance.context = context;\n\n    await eventInstance.teleport({ x, y });\n    await eventInstance.execMethod('onInit');\n\n    this.events()[id] = eventInstance;\n    this.setEventRuntimeMetadata(id, effectiveMode, scenarioOwnerId);\n    return id;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get an event by its ID\n   * \n   * Returns the event with the specified ID, or undefined if not found.\n   * The return type can be narrowed using TypeScript generics.\n   * \n   * @param eventId - The unique identifier of the event\n   * @returns The event instance, or undefined if not found\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Get any event\n   * const event = map.getEvent('npc-1');\n   * \n   * // Get event with type narrowing\n   * const npc = map.getEvent<MyNPC>('npc-1');\n   * if (npc) {\n   *   npc.speak('Hello!');\n   * }\n   * ```\n   */\n  getEvent<T extends RpgPlayer>(eventId: string): T | undefined {\n    return this.events()[eventId] as T\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get a player by their ID\n   * \n   * Returns the player with the specified ID, or undefined if not found.\n   * \n   * @param playerId - The unique identifier of the player\n   * @returns The player instance, or undefined if not found\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * const player = map.getPlayer('player-123');\n   * if (player) {\n   *   console.log(`Player ${player.name} is on the map`);\n   * }\n   * ```\n   */\n  getPlayer(playerId: string): RpgPlayer | undefined {\n    return this.players()[playerId]\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get all players currently on the map\n   * \n   * Returns an array of all players that are currently connected to this map.\n   * \n   * @returns Array of all RpgPlayer instances on the map\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * const players = map.getPlayers();\n   * console.log(`There are ${players.length} players on the map`);\n   * \n   * players.forEach(player => {\n   *   console.log(`- ${player.name}`);\n   * });\n   * ```\n   */\n  getPlayers(): RpgPlayer[] {\n    return Object.values(this.players())\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get all events on the map\n   * \n   * Returns an array of all events (NPCs, objects, etc.) that are currently\n   * on this map.\n   * \n   * @returns Array of all RpgEvent instances on the map\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * const events = map.getEvents();\n   * console.log(`There are ${events.length} events on the map`);\n   * \n   * events.forEach(event => {\n   *   console.log(`- ${event.name} at (${event.x}, ${event.y})`);\n   * });\n   * ```\n   */\n  getEvents(): RpgEvent[] {\n    return Object.values(this.events())\n  }\n\n  getEventsForPlayer(playerOrId: string | RpgPlayer): RpgEvent[] {\n    return this.getEvents().filter(event => this.isEventVisibleForPlayer(event, playerOrId));\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get the first event that matches a condition\n   * \n   * Searches through all events on the map and returns the first one that\n   * matches the provided callback function.\n   * \n   * @param cb - Callback function that returns true for the desired event\n   * @returns The first matching event, or undefined if none found\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Find an event by name\n   * const npc = map.getEventBy(event => event.name === 'Merchant');\n   * \n   * // Find an event at a specific position\n   * const chest = map.getEventBy(event => \n   *   event.x === 100 && event.y === 200\n   * );\n   * ```\n   */\n  getEventBy(cb: (event: RpgEvent) => boolean): RpgEvent | undefined {\n    return this.getEventsBy(cb)[0]\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get all events that match a condition\n   * \n   * Searches through all events on the map and returns all events that\n   * match the provided callback function.\n   * \n   * @param cb - Callback function that returns true for desired events\n   * @returns Array of all matching events\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Find all NPCs\n   * const npcs = map.getEventsBy(event => event.name.startsWith('NPC-'));\n   * \n   * // Find all events in a specific area\n   * const nearbyEvents = map.getEventsBy(event => \n   *   event.x >= 0 && event.x <= 100 &&\n   *   event.y >= 0 && event.y <= 100\n   * );\n   * ```\n   */\n  getEventsBy(cb: (event: RpgEvent) => boolean): RpgEvent[] {\n    return this.getEvents().filter(cb)\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Remove an event from the map\n   * \n   * Removes the event with the specified ID from the map. The event will\n   * be removed from the synchronized events signal, causing it to disappear\n   * on all clients.\n   * \n   * @param eventId - The unique identifier of the event to remove\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Remove an event\n   * map.removeEvent('npc-1');\n   * \n   * // Remove event after interaction\n   * const chest = map.getEvent('chest-1');\n   * if (chest) {\n   *   // ... do something with chest ...\n   *   map.removeEvent('chest-1');\n   * }\n   * ```\n   */\n  removeEvent(eventId: string) {\n    const event = this.getEvent(eventId) as any;\n    if (event) {\n      try {\n        event.stopMoveTo?.();\n      }\n      catch {\n        // Ignore teardown race: the physics entity may already be gone.\n      }\n      try {\n        event.breakRoutes?.(true);\n      }\n      catch {\n        // Ignore teardown race in route manager.\n      }\n    }\n    this.clearEventRuntimeMetadata(eventId);\n    delete this.events()[eventId]\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Display a component animation at a specific position on the map\n   * \n   * This method broadcasts a component animation to all clients connected to the map,\n   * allowing temporary visual effects to be displayed at any location on the map.\n   * Component animations are custom Canvas Engine components that can display\n   * complex effects with custom logic and parameters.\n   * \n   * @param id - The ID of the component animation to display\n   * @param position - The x, y coordinates where to display the animation\n   * @param params - Parameters to pass to the component animation\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Show explosion at specific coordinates\n   * map.showComponentAnimation(\"explosion\", { x: 300, y: 400 }, {\n   *   intensity: 2.5,\n   *   duration: 1500\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Show area damage effect\n   * map.showComponentAnimation(\"area-damage\", { x: player.x, y: player.y }, {\n   *   radius: 100,\n   *   color: \"red\",\n   *   damage: 50\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Show treasure spawn effect\n   * map.showComponentAnimation(\"treasure-spawn\", { x: 150, y: 200 }, {\n   *   sparkle: true,\n   *   sound: \"treasure-appear\"\n   * });\n   * ```\n   */\n  showComponentAnimation(id: string, position: { x: number, y: number }, params: any) {\n    this.$broadcast({\n      type: \"showComponentAnimation\",\n      value: {\n        id,\n        params,\n        position,\n      },\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Display a spritesheet animation at a specific position on the map\n   * \n   * This method displays a temporary visual animation using a spritesheet at any\n   * location on the map. It's a convenience method that internally uses showComponentAnimation\n   * with the built-in 'animation' component. This is useful for spell effects, environmental\n   * animations, or any visual feedback that uses predefined spritesheets.\n   * \n   * @param position - The x, y coordinates where to display the animation\n   * @param graphic - The ID of the spritesheet to use for the animation\n   * @param animationName - The name of the animation within the spritesheet (default: 'default')\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Show explosion at specific coordinates\n   * map.showAnimation({ x: 100, y: 200 }, \"explosion\");\n   * \n   * // Show spell effect at player position\n   * const playerPos = { x: player.x, y: player.y };\n   * map.showAnimation(playerPos, \"spell-effects\", \"lightning\");\n   * \n   * // Show environmental effect\n   * map.showAnimation({ x: 300, y: 150 }, \"nature-effects\", \"wind-gust\");\n   * \n   * // Show portal opening animation\n   * map.showAnimation({ x: 500, y: 400 }, \"portals\", \"opening\");\n   * ```\n   */\n  showAnimation(position: { x: number, y: number }, graphic: string, animationName: string = 'default') {\n    this.showComponentAnimation('animation', position, {\n      graphic,\n      animationName,\n    })\n  }\n\n  private cloneWeatherState(weather: WeatherState | null): WeatherState | null {\n    if (!weather) {\n      return null;\n    }\n    return {\n      ...weather,\n      params: weather.params ? { ...weather.params } : undefined,\n    };\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get the current map weather state.\n   */\n  getWeather(): WeatherState | null {\n    return this.cloneWeatherState(this._weatherState);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Set the full weather state for this map.\n   *\n   * When `sync` is true (default), all connected clients receive the new weather.\n   */\n  setWeather(next: WeatherState | null, options: WeatherSetOptions = {}): WeatherState | null {\n    const sync = options.sync !== false;\n    if (next && !next.effect) {\n      throw new Error(\"setWeather: 'effect' is required when weather is not null.\");\n    }\n    this._weatherState = this.cloneWeatherState(next);\n    if (sync) {\n      this.$broadcast({\n        type: \"weatherState\",\n        value: this._weatherState,\n      });\n    }\n    return this.getWeather();\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Patch the current weather state.\n   *\n   * Nested `params` values are merged.\n   */\n  patchWeather(patch: Partial<WeatherState>, options: WeatherSetOptions = {}): WeatherState | null {\n    const current = this._weatherState ?? null;\n    if (!current && !patch.effect) {\n      throw new Error(\"patchWeather: 'effect' is required when no weather is currently set.\");\n    }\n    const next: WeatherState = {\n      ...(current ?? {}),\n      ...patch,\n      params: {\n        ...(current?.params ?? {}),\n        ...(patch.params ?? {}),\n      },\n    } as WeatherState;\n    return this.setWeather(next, options);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Clear weather for this map.\n   */\n  clearWeather(options: WeatherSetOptions = {}): void {\n    this.setWeather(null, options);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Configure runtime synchronized properties on the map\n   * \n   * This method allows you to dynamically add synchronized properties to the map\n   * that will be automatically synced with clients. The schema follows the same\n   * structure as module properties with `$initial`, `$syncWithClient`, and `$permanent` options.\n   * \n   * ## Architecture\n   * \n   * - Reads a schema object shaped like module props\n   * - Creates typed sync signals with @signe/sync\n   * - Properties are accessible as `map.propertyName`\n   * \n   * @param schema - Schema object defining the properties to sync\n   * @param schema[key].$initial - Initial value for the property\n   * @param schema[key].$syncWithClient - Whether to sync this property to clients\n   * @param schema[key].$permanent - Whether to persist this property\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Add synchronized properties to the map\n   * map.setSync({\n   *   weather: {\n   *     $initial: 'sunny',\n   *     $syncWithClient: true,\n   *     $permanent: false\n   *   },\n   *   timeOfDay: {\n   *     $initial: 12,\n   *     $syncWithClient: true,\n   *     $permanent: false\n   *   }\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Use the properties\n   * map.weather.set('rainy');\n   * const currentWeather = map.weather();\n   * ```\n   */\n  setSync(schema: Record<string, any>) {\n    for (let key in schema) {\n      const initial = typeof schema[key]?.$initial !== 'undefined' ? schema[key].$initial : null;\n      // Use type() directly with a plain object holder to avoid signal type mismatch\n      const holder: any = {};\n      this[key] = type(signal(initial) as any, key, {\n        syncToClient: schema[key]?.$syncWithClient,\n        persist: schema[key]?.$permanent,\n      }, holder);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Apply sync to the client\n   * \n   * This method applies sync to the client by calling the `$applySync()` method.\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * map.applySyncToClient();\n   * ```\n   */\n  applySyncToClient() {\n    this.$applySync();\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Create a shape dynamically on the map\n   * \n   * This method creates a static hitbox on the map that can be used for\n   * collision detection, area triggers, or visual boundaries. The shape is\n   * backed by the physics engine's static entity system for accurate collision detection.\n   * \n   * ## Architecture\n   * \n   * Creates a static entity (hitbox) in the physics engine at the specified position and size.\n   * The shape is stored internally and can be retrieved by name. When players or events\n   * collide with this hitbox, the `onInShape` and `onOutShape` hooks are automatically\n   * triggered on both the player and the event.\n   * \n   * @param obj - Shape configuration object\n   * @param obj.x - X position of the shape (top-left corner) (required)\n   * @param obj.y - Y position of the shape (top-left corner) (required)\n   * @param obj.width - Width of the shape in pixels (required)\n   * @param obj.height - Height of the shape in pixels (required)\n   * @param obj.name - Name of the shape (optional, auto-generated if not provided)\n   * @param obj.z - Z position/depth for rendering (optional)\n   * @param obj.color - Color in hexadecimal format, shared with client (optional)\n   * @param obj.collision - Whether the shape has collision (optional)\n   * @param obj.properties - Additional custom properties (optional)\n   * @returns The created RpgShape instance\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Create a simple rectangular shape\n   * const shape = map.createShape({\n   *   x: 100,\n   *   y: 200,\n   *   width: 50,\n   *   height: 50,\n   *   name: \"spawn-zone\"\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Create a shape with visual properties\n   * const triggerZone = map.createShape({\n   *   x: 300,\n   *   y: 400,\n   *   width: 100,\n   *   height: 100,\n   *   name: \"treasure-area\",\n   *   color: \"#FFD700\",\n   *   z: 1,\n   *   collision: false,\n   *   properties: {\n   *     type: \"treasure\",\n   *     value: 100\n   *   }\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Player hooks will be triggered automatically\n   * const player: RpgPlayerHooks = {\n   *   onInShape(player: RpgPlayer, shape: RpgShape) {\n   *     console.log('in', player.name, shape.name);\n   *   },\n   *   onOutShape(player: RpgPlayer, shape: RpgShape) {\n   *     console.log('out', player.name, shape.name);\n   *   }\n   * };\n   * ```\n   */\n  createShape(obj: {\n    x: number;\n    y: number;\n    width: number;\n    height: number;\n    name?: string;\n    z?: number;\n    color?: string;\n    collision?: boolean;\n    properties?: Record<string, any>;\n  }): RpgShape {\n    const { x, y, width, height } = obj;\n\n    // Validate required parameters\n    if (typeof x !== 'number' || typeof y !== 'number') {\n      throw new Error('Shape x and y must be numbers');\n    }\n    if (typeof width !== 'number' || width <= 0) {\n      throw new Error('Shape width must be a positive number');\n    }\n    if (typeof height !== 'number' || height <= 0) {\n      throw new Error('Shape height must be a positive number');\n    }\n\n    // Generate name if not provided\n    const name = obj.name || generateShortUUID();\n\n    // Check if shape with this name already exists\n    if (this._shapes.has(name)) {\n      throw new Error(`Shape with name \"${name}\" already exists`);\n    }\n\n    // Calculate center position for the static hitbox\n    const centerX = x + width / 2;\n    const centerY = y + height / 2;\n\n    // Create static entity (hitbox) in physics engine\n    const entityId = `shape-${name}`;\n    const entity = this.physic.createEntity({\n      uuid: entityId,\n      position: { x: centerX, y: centerY },\n      width: width,\n      height: height,\n      mass: Infinity, // Static entity\n      state: EntityState.Static,\n      restitution: 0, // No bounce\n    });\n    entity.freeze(); // Ensure it's frozen\n\n    // Build properties object\n    const properties: Record<string, any> = {\n      ...(obj.properties || {}),\n    };\n    if (obj.z !== undefined) properties.z = obj.z;\n    if (obj.color !== undefined) properties.color = obj.color;\n    if (obj.collision !== undefined) properties.collision = obj.collision;\n\n    // Create RpgShape instance\n    // Note: We use entityId as physicZoneId for compatibility, but it's actually an entity UUID\n    const shape = new RpgShape({\n      name: name,\n      positioning: 'default',\n      width: width,\n      height: height,\n      x: centerX,\n      y: centerY,\n      properties: properties,\n      playerOwner: undefined, // Static shapes are not attached to players\n      physicZoneId: entityId, // Store entity UUID for reference\n      map: this,\n    });\n\n    // Store the shape\n    this._shapes.set(name, shape);\n    this._shapeEntities.set(entityId, shape);\n\n    return shape;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Delete a shape from the map\n   * \n   * Removes a shape by its name and cleans up the associated static hitbox entity.\n   * If the shape doesn't exist, the method does nothing.\n   * \n   * @param name - Name of the shape to remove\n   * @returns void\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Create and then remove a shape\n   * const shape = map.createShape({\n   *   x: 100,\n   *   y: 200,\n   *   width: 50,\n   *   height: 50,\n   *   name: \"temp-zone\"\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Later, remove it\n   * map.removeShape(\"temp-zone\");\n   * ```\n   */\n  removeShape(name: string): void {\n    const shape = this._shapes.get(name);\n    if (!shape) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Remove entity from physics engine\n    const entityId = (shape as any)._physicZoneId;\n    const entity = this.physic.getEntityByUUID(entityId);\n    if (entity) {\n      this.physic.removeEntity(entity);\n    }\n\n    // Remove from internal storage\n    this._shapes.delete(name);\n    this._shapeEntities.delete(entityId);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get all shapes on the map\n   * \n   * Returns an array of all shapes that have been created on this map,\n   * regardless of whether they are static shapes or player-attached shapes.\n   * \n   * @returns Array of RpgShape instances\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Create multiple shapes\n   * map.createShape({ x: 0, y: 0, width: 50, height: 50, name: \"zone1\" });\n   * map.createShape({ x: 100, y: 100, width: 50, height: 50, name: \"zone2\" });\n   * \n   * // Get all shapes\n   * const allShapes = map.getShapes();\n   * console.log(allShapes.length); // 2\n   * ```\n   */\n  getShapes(): RpgShape[] {\n    return Array.from(this._shapes.values());\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get a shape by its name\n   * \n   * Returns a shape with the specified name, or undefined if no shape\n   * with that name exists on the map.\n   * \n   * @param name - Name of the shape to retrieve\n   * @returns The RpgShape instance, or undefined if not found\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Create a shape with a specific name\n   * map.createShape({\n   *   x: 100,\n   *   y: 200,\n   *   width: 50,\n   *   height: 50,\n   *   name: \"spawn-point\"\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Retrieve it later\n   * const spawnZone = map.getShape(\"spawn-point\");\n   * if (spawnZone) {\n   *   console.log(`Spawn zone at (${spawnZone.x}, ${spawnZone.y})`);\n   * }\n   * ```\n   */\n  getShape(name: string): RpgShape | undefined {\n    return this._shapes.get(name);\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Play a sound for all players on the map\n   * \n   * This method plays a sound for all players currently on the map by iterating\n   * over each player and calling `player.playSound()`. The sound must be defined\n   * on the client side (in the client module configuration).\n   * This is ideal for environmental sounds, battle music, or map-wide events that\n   * all players should hear simultaneously.\n   * \n   * ## Design\n   * \n   * Iterates over all players on the map and calls `player.playSound()` for each one.\n   * This avoids code duplication and reuses the existing player sound logic.\n   * For player-specific sounds, use `player.playSound()` directly.\n   * \n   * @param soundId - Sound identifier, defined on the client side\n   * @param options - Optional sound configuration\n   * @param options.volume - Volume level (0.0 to 1.0, default: 1.0)\n   * @param options.loop - Whether the sound should loop (default: false)\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Play a sound for all players on the map\n   * map.playSound(\"explosion\");\n   * \n   * // Play background music for everyone with volume and loop\n   * map.playSound(\"battle-theme\", {\n   *   volume: 0.7,\n   *   loop: true\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Play a door opening sound at low volume\n   * map.playSound(\"door-open\", { volume: 0.4 });\n   * ```\n   */\n  playSound(soundId: string, options?: { volume?: number; loop?: boolean }): void {\n    const players = this.getPlayers();\n    players.forEach((player) => {\n      player.playSound(soundId, options);\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Stop a sound for all players on the map\n   * \n   * This method stops a sound that was previously started with `map.playSound()`\n   * for all players on the map by iterating over each player and calling `player.stopSound()`.\n   * \n   * @param soundId - Sound identifier to stop\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Start background music for everyone\n   * map.playSound(\"battle-theme\", { loop: true });\n   * \n   * // Later, stop it for everyone\n   * map.stopSound(\"battle-theme\");\n   * ```\n   */\n  stopSound(soundId: string): void {\n    const players = this.getPlayers();\n    players.forEach((player) => {\n      player.stopSound(soundId);\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Shake the map for all players\n   * \n   * This method triggers a shake animation on the map for all players currently on the map.\n   * The shake effect creates a visual feedback that can be used for earthquakes, explosions,\n   * impacts, or any dramatic event that should affect the entire map visually.\n   * \n   * ## Architecture\n   * \n   * Broadcasts a shake event to all clients connected to the map. Each client receives\n   * the shake configuration and triggers the shake animation on the map container using\n   * Canvas Engine's shake directive.\n   * \n   * @param options - Optional shake configuration\n   * @param options.intensity - Shake intensity in pixels (default: 10)\n   * @param options.duration - Duration of the shake animation in milliseconds (default: 500)\n   * @param options.frequency - Number of shake oscillations during the animation (default: 10)\n   * @param options.direction - Direction of the shake - 'x', 'y', or 'both' (default: 'both')\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Basic shake with default settings\n   * map.shakeMap();\n   * \n   * // Intense earthquake effect\n   * map.shakeMap({\n   *   intensity: 25,\n   *   duration: 1000,\n   *   frequency: 15,\n   *   direction: 'both'\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Horizontal shake for side impact\n   * map.shakeMap({\n   *   intensity: 15,\n   *   duration: 400,\n   *   direction: 'x'\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Vertical shake for ground impact\n   * map.shakeMap({\n   *   intensity: 20,\n   *   duration: 600,\n   *   direction: 'y'\n   * });\n   * ```\n   */\n  shakeMap(options?: {\n    intensity?: number;\n    duration?: number;\n    frequency?: number;\n    direction?: 'x' | 'y' | 'both';\n  }): void {\n    this.$broadcast({\n      type: \"shakeMap\",\n      value: {\n        intensity: options?.intensity ?? 10,\n        duration: options?.duration ?? 500,\n        frequency: options?.frequency ?? 10,\n        direction: options?.direction ?? 'both',\n      },\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Clear all server resources and reset state\n   * \n   * This method should be called to clean up all server-side resources when\n   * shutting down or resetting the map. It stops the input processing loop\n   * and ensures that all subscriptions are properly cleaned up.\n   * \n   * ## Design\n   * \n   * This method is used primarily in testing environments to ensure clean\n   * state between tests. It stops the tick subscription to prevent memory leaks.\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // In test cleanup\n   * afterEach(() => {\n   *   map.clear();\n   * });\n   * ```\n   */\n  clear(): void {\n    try {\n      // Stop input processing loop\n      if (this._inputLoopSubscription) {\n        this._inputLoopSubscription.unsubscribe();\n        this._inputLoopSubscription = undefined;\n      }\n    } catch (error) {\n      console.warn('Error during map cleanup:', error);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\nexport interface RpgMap extends RoomMethods { }\n","import { inject } from \"@signe/di\";\nimport { Action, MockConnection, Room } from \"@signe/room\";\nimport { Hooks, ModulesToken } from \"@rpgjs/common\";\nimport { context } from \"../core/context\";\nimport { users } from \"@signe/sync\";\nimport { signal } from \"@signe/reactive\";\nimport { RpgPlayer } from \"../Player/Player\";\nimport { BaseRoom } from \"./BaseRoom\";\nimport { buildSaveSlotMeta, resolveSaveStorageStrategy } from \"../services/save\";\nimport { lastValueFrom } from \"rxjs\";\n\n@Room({\n  path: \"lobby-{id}\",\n})\nexport class LobbyRoom extends BaseRoom {\n  @users(RpgPlayer) players = signal({});\n  autoSync: boolean = true;\n\n  constructor(room) {\n    super();\n    const isTest = room.env.TEST === 'true' ? true : false;\n    if (isTest) {\n      this.autoSync = false;\n    }\n  }\n\n  async onJoin(player: RpgPlayer, conn: MockConnection) {\n    player.map = this;\n    player.context = context;\n    player.conn = conn;\n    this.hooks.callHooks(\"server-player-onConnected\", player).subscribe();\n  }\n\n  @Action('gui.interaction')\n  async guiInteraction(player: RpgPlayer, value: { guiId: string, name: string, data: any }) {\n    const id = value.data.id\n    if (id === 'start') {\n      player.initializeDefaultStats();\n      this.hooks.callHooks(\"server-player-onStart\", player).subscribe();\n    }\n  }\n}\n","import { Server } from \"@signe/room\";\nimport { RpgMap } from \"./rooms/map\";\nimport { LobbyRoom } from \"./rooms/lobby\";\n\nexport class RpgServerEngine extends Server {\n  rooms = [RpgMap, LobbyRoom];\n}\n","import { injector, Providers } from \"@signe/di\";\nimport { RpgServerEngine } from \"../RpgServerEngine\";\nimport { context } from \"./context\";\nimport { setInject } from \"./inject\";\n\ninterface SetupOptions {\n  providers: Providers;\n}\n\nexport function createServer(options: SetupOptions): any {\n  return class extends RpgServerEngine {\n    config = options;\n    \n    async onStart() {\n      setInject(context);\n      await injector(context, options.providers);\n      return super.onStart();\n    }\n  };\n}","/**\n * Component definitions for player UI elements\n * \n * This module provides factory functions to create component definitions\n * that can be displayed above or below player graphics. Components are\n * synchronized from server to client and rendered using CanvasEngine.\n * \n * ## Design\n * \n * Components are defined as data structures that describe UI elements\n * (text, bars, shapes) with their properties and layout options. The\n * server creates these definitions and they are automatically synchronized\n * to all clients on the map.\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * import { Components } from '@rpgjs/server';\n * \n * // Create a text component\n * const nameComponent = Components.text('{name}');\n * \n * // Create an HP bar\n * const hpBar = Components.hpBar();\n * \n * // Set components on player\n * player.setComponentsTop([nameComponent, hpBar]);\n * ```\n */\n\nexport interface ComponentLayout {\n  /** Width of the component block in pixels */\n  width?: number;\n  /** Height of the component block in pixels */\n  height?: number;\n  /** Margin from the top of the player in pixels */\n  marginTop?: number;\n  /** Margin from the bottom of the player in pixels */\n  marginBottom?: number;\n  /** Margin from the left of the player in pixels */\n  marginLeft?: number;\n  /** Margin from the right of the player in pixels */\n  marginRight?: number;\n}\n\nexport interface TextComponentOptions {\n  /** Text color in hexadecimal format (e.g., '#000000') */\n  fill?: string;\n  /** Font size in pixels */\n  fontSize?: number;\n  /** Font family */\n  fontFamily?: string;\n  /** Font style: 'normal', 'italic', 'oblique' */\n  fontStyle?: 'normal' | 'italic' | 'oblique';\n  /** Font weight: 'normal', 'bold', 'bolder', 'lighter', or numeric values */\n  fontWeight?: 'normal' | 'bold' | 'bolder' | 'lighter' | '100' | '200' | '300' | '400' | '500' | '600' | '700' | '800' | '900' | number;\n  /** Stroke color in hexadecimal format */\n  stroke?: string;\n  /** Opacity between 0 and 1 */\n  opacity?: number;\n  /** Word wrap */\n  wordWrap?: boolean;\n  /** Text alignment */\n  align?: 'left' | 'center' | 'right' | 'justify';\n}\n\nexport interface BarComponentOptions {\n  /** Background color in hexadecimal format */\n  bgColor?: string;\n  /** Fill color in hexadecimal format */\n  fillColor?: string;\n  /** Border color in hexadecimal format */\n  borderColor?: string;\n  /** Border width */\n  borderWidth?: number;\n  /** Height of the bar in pixels */\n  height?: number;\n  /** Width of the bar in pixels */\n  width?: number;\n  /** Border radius */\n  borderRadius?: number;\n  /** Opacity between 0 and 1 */\n  opacity?: number;\n}\n\nexport interface ShapeComponentOptions {\n  /** Fill color in hexadecimal format */\n  fill: string;\n  /** Type of shape */\n  type: 'circle' | 'rectangle' | 'ellipse' | 'polygon' | 'line' | 'rounded-rectangle';\n  /** Radius (for circle) */\n  radius?: number | string;\n  /** Width (for rectangle, ellipse) */\n  width?: number | string;\n  /** Height (for rectangle, ellipse) */\n  height?: number | string;\n  /** X1 position (for line) */\n  x1?: number | string;\n  /** Y1 position (for line) */\n  y1?: number | string;\n  /** X2 position (for line) */\n  x2?: number | string;\n  /** Y2 position (for line) */\n  y2?: number | string;\n  /** Points array (for polygon) */\n  points?: number[];\n  /** Opacity between 0 and 1 */\n  opacity?: number | string;\n  /** Line/border style */\n  line?: {\n    color?: string;\n    width?: number;\n    alpha?: number;\n  };\n}\n\nexport type ComponentDefinition = \n  | { type: 'text'; value: string; style?: TextComponentOptions }\n  | { type: 'hpBar'; style?: BarComponentOptions; text?: string | null }\n  | { type: 'spBar'; style?: BarComponentOptions; text?: string | null }\n  | { type: 'bar'; current: string; max: string; style?: BarComponentOptions; text?: string | null }\n  | { type: 'shape'; value: ShapeComponentOptions }\n  | { type: 'image'; value: string }\n  | { type: 'tile'; value: number | string };\n\nexport type ComponentInput = ComponentDefinition | ComponentDefinition[] | ComponentDefinition[][];\n\n/**\n * Components factory for creating component definitions\n * \n * Provides factory methods to create various UI components that can be\n * displayed above or below player graphics. Components support template\n * strings with placeholders like {name}, {hp}, etc. that are replaced\n * with actual player property values on the client.\n * \n * @example\n * ```ts\n * // Create a text component\n * Components.text('Player: {name}');\n * \n * // Create an HP bar with custom text\n * Components.hpBar({}, '{$percent}%');\n * \n * // Create a custom bar\n * Components.bar('wood', 'param.maxWood');\n * ```\n */\nexport const Components = {\n  /**\n   * Create a text component\n   * \n   * Creates a text component that displays text with optional styling.\n   * Supports template strings with placeholders like {name}, {hp}, etc.\n   * that are replaced with actual player property values.\n   * \n   * ## Design\n   * \n   * Text components use template strings to allow dynamic content without\n   * resending the entire component structure when values change. Only the\n   * property values are synchronized, reducing bandwidth usage.\n   * \n   * @param text - Text to display, can include placeholders like {name}, {hp}\n   * @param options - Text styling options\n   * @returns Component definition for text\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Simple text\n   * Components.text('Player Name');\n   * \n   * // Text with placeholder\n   * Components.text('{name}');\n   * \n   * // Text with styling\n   * Components.text('{name}', {\n   *   fill: '#000000',\n   *   fontSize: 20\n   * });\n   * ```\n   */\n  text(value: string, style?: TextComponentOptions): ComponentDefinition {\n    return {\n      type: 'text',\n      value,\n      style\n    };\n  },\n\n  /**\n   * Create an HP bar component\n   * \n   * Creates a health point bar that automatically displays the player's\n   * current HP relative to their maximum HP. The bar updates automatically\n   * as HP changes.\n   * \n   * ## Design\n   * \n   * HP bars read from the player's hp and param.maxHp properties. The\n   * bar can optionally display text above it showing current, max, or\n   * percentage values.\n   * \n   * @param options - Bar styling options\n   * @param text - Optional text to display above the bar. Can use placeholders:\n   *   - {$current} - Current HP value\n   *   - {$max} - Maximum HP value\n   *   - {$percent} - Percentage value\n   *   Set to null to hide text\n   * @returns Component definition for HP bar\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Simple HP bar\n   * Components.hpBar();\n   * \n   * // HP bar with percentage text\n   * Components.hpBar({}, '{$percent}%');\n   * \n   * // HP bar with custom styling\n   * Components.hpBar({\n   *   fillColor: '#ff0000',\n   *   height: 8\n   * });\n   * ```\n   */\n  hpBar(style?: BarComponentOptions, text?: string | null): ComponentDefinition {\n    return {\n      type: 'hpBar',\n      style,\n      text: text ?? undefined\n    };\n  },\n\n  /**\n   * Create an SP bar component\n   * \n   * Creates a skill point bar that automatically displays the player's\n   * current SP relative to their maximum SP. The bar updates automatically\n   * as SP changes.\n   * \n   * @param style - Bar styling options\n   * @param text - Optional text to display above the bar. Can use placeholders:\n   *   - {$current} - Current SP value\n   *   - {$max} - Maximum SP value\n   *   - {$percent} - Percentage value\n   *   Set to null to hide text\n   * @returns Component definition for SP bar\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Simple SP bar\n   * Components.spBar();\n   * \n   * // SP bar with text\n   * Components.spBar({}, 'SP: {$current}/{$max}');\n   * ```\n   */\n  spBar(style?: BarComponentOptions, text?: string | null): ComponentDefinition {\n    return {\n      type: 'spBar',\n      style,\n      text: text ?? undefined\n    };\n  },\n\n  /**\n   * Create a custom bar component\n   * \n   * Creates a bar that displays a custom property value relative to a maximum.\n   * Useful for displaying custom resources like wood, mana, energy, etc.\n   * \n   * @param current - Property path for current value (e.g., 'wood', 'mana')\n   * @param max - Property path for maximum value (e.g., 'param.maxWood', 'param.maxMana')\n   * @param style - Bar styling options\n   * @param text - Optional text to display above the bar. Can use placeholders:\n   *   - {$current} - Current value\n   *   - {$max} - Maximum value\n   *   - {$percent} - Percentage value\n   *   Set to null to hide text\n   * @returns Component definition for custom bar\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Bar for custom property\n   * Components.bar('wood', 'param.maxWood');\n   * \n   * // Bar with text\n   * Components.bar('mana', 'param.maxMana', {}, 'Mana: {$current}/{$max}');\n   * ```\n   */\n  bar(current: string, max: string, style?: BarComponentOptions, text?: string | null): ComponentDefinition {\n    return {\n      type: 'bar',\n      current,\n      max,\n      style,\n      text: text ?? undefined\n    };\n  },\n\n  /**\n   * Create a shape component\n   * \n   * Creates a geometric shape that can be displayed above or below the player.\n   * Useful for visual indicators, backgrounds, or decorative elements.\n   * \n   * @param value - Shape configuration options\n   * @returns Component definition for shape\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * // Circle shape\n   * Components.shape({\n   *   fill: '#ffffff',\n   *   type: 'circle',\n   *   radius: 10\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Rectangle shape\n   * Components.shape({\n   *   fill: '#ff0000',\n   *   type: 'rectangle',\n   *   width: 32,\n   *   height: 32\n   * });\n   * \n   * // Using parameters\n   * Components.shape({\n   *   fill: '#ffffff',\n   *   type: 'circle',\n   *   radius: 'hp' // radius will be the same as hp value\n   * });\n   * ```\n   */\n  shape(value: ShapeComponentOptions): ComponentDefinition {\n    return {\n      type: 'shape',\n      value\n    };\n  },\n\n  /**\n   * Create an image component\n   * \n   * Displays an image from a URL or spritesheet identifier.\n   * \n   * @param value - Image source URL or spritesheet identifier\n   * @returns Component definition for image\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * Components.image('mygraphic.png');\n   * ```\n   */\n  image(value: string): ComponentDefinition {\n    return {\n      type: 'image',\n      value\n    };\n  },\n\n  /**\n   * Create a tile component\n   * \n   * Displays a tile from a tileset by ID.\n   * \n   * @param value - Tile ID in the tileset\n   * @returns Component definition for tile\n   * \n   * @example\n   * ```ts\n   * Components.tile(3); // Use tile #3\n   * ```\n   */\n  tile(value: number | string): ComponentDefinition {\n    return {\n      type: 'tile',\n      value\n    };\n  }\n}; \n\n","import type { SaveSlot, SaveSlotEntries, SaveSlotList, SaveSlotMeta } from \"@rpgjs/common\";\nimport type { SaveStorageStrategy } from \"../services/save\";\nimport { RpgPlayer } from \"../Player/Player\";\n\nexport interface LocalStorageSaveStorageOptions {\n  key?: string;\n}\n\n/**\n * Save storage strategy backed by browser localStorage.\n *\n * Intended for standalone mode where the server runs in the browser\n * and localStorage is available.\n */\nexport class LocalStorageSaveStorageStrategy implements SaveStorageStrategy {\n  private key: string;\n\n  constructor(options: LocalStorageSaveStorageOptions = {}) {\n    this.key = options.key ?? \"rpgjs-save-slots\";\n  }\n\n  async list(_player: RpgPlayer): Promise<SaveSlotList> {\n    return this.stripSnapshots(this.readSlots());\n  }\n\n  async get(_player: RpgPlayer, index: number): Promise<SaveSlot | null> {\n    const slots = this.readSlots();\n    return slots[index] ?? null;\n  }\n\n  async save(_player: RpgPlayer, index: number, snapshot: string, meta: SaveSlotMeta): Promise<void> {\n    const slots = this.readSlots();\n    const existing = slots[index];\n    slots[index] = {\n      ...(existing ?? {}),\n      ...meta,\n      snapshot,\n    };\n    this.writeSlots(slots);\n  }\n\n  async delete(_player: RpgPlayer, index: number): Promise<void> {\n    const slots = this.readSlots();\n    slots[index] = null;\n    this.writeSlots(slots);\n  }\n\n  private readSlots(): SaveSlotEntries {\n    if (typeof localStorage === \"undefined\") {\n      return [];\n    }\n    const raw = localStorage.getItem(this.key);\n    if (!raw) return [];\n    try {\n      const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);\n      return Array.isArray(parsed) ? parsed : [];\n    } catch {\n      return [];\n    }\n  }\n\n  private writeSlots(slots: SaveSlotEntries) {\n    if (typeof localStorage === \"undefined\") {\n      return;\n    }\n    localStorage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(slots));\n  }\n\n  private stripSnapshots(slots: SaveSlotEntries): SaveSlotList {\n    return slots.map((slot) => {\n      if (!slot) return null;\n      const { snapshot, ...meta } = slot;\n      return meta;\n    });\n  }\n}\n","import type { WeatherState } from \"@rpgjs/common\";\nimport type { MapEventDefinition, MapEventPlacement } from \"../rooms/map\";\n\nexport interface MapOptions {\n    /** \n     * Map identifier. Allows to go to the map (for example with player.changeMap())\n     * \n     * @prop {string} [id]\n     * @memberof MapData\n     * */\n    id?: string,\n\n    /** \n    * the path to the .tmx file (Tiled Map Editor)\n    * \n    * Remember to use `require()` function\n    * \n    * ```ts\n    * import { MapData, RpgMap } from '@rpgjs/server'\n    * \n    * @MapData({\n    *      id: 'town',\n    *      file: require('./tmx/town.tmx')\n    * })\n    * class TownMap extends RpgMap { } \n    * ``` \n    * @prop {string} file\n    * @memberof MapData\n    * */\n    file?: any,\n\n    /** \n     * The name of the map.\n     * @prop {string} [name]\n     * @memberof MapData\n     * */\n    name?: string,\n\n    /** \n    * Map events. This is an array containing `RpgEvent` classes. \n    * You can also give an object that will indicate the positions of the event on the map.\n    * \n    * ```ts\n    * import { MapData, RpgMap, EventData, RpgEvent } from '@rpgjs/server'\n    * \n    * @EventData({\n    *      name: 'Ev-1'\n    * })\n    * class NpcEvent extends RpgEvent { }\n    * \n    * @MapData({\n    *      id: 'medieval',\n    *      file: require('./tmx/town.tmx'),\n    *      events: [NpcEvent]\n    * })\n    * class TownMap extends RpgMap {}\n    * ```\n    * \n    * If the positions are not defined, the event will be placed on a Tiled Map Editor shape ([/guide/create-event.html#position-the-event-on-the-map](Guide)). Otherwise, it will be placed at `{x:0, y:0 }`\n    * \n    * You can give positions:\n    * \n    * ```ts\n    * events: [{ event: NpcEvent, x: 10, y: 30 }]\n    * ```\n    *\n    * For object-based events, put the hooks in the `event` property and keep map placement\n    * (`x`, `y`, `id`) at the wrapper level:\n    *\n    * ```ts\n    * events: [{\n    *   x: 200,\n    *   y: 120,\n    *   event: {\n    *     onInit() {\n    *       this.setGraphic('female')\n    *     }\n    *   }\n    * }]\n    * ```\n    * \n    * @prop {(MapEventDefinition | MapEventPlacement)[]} [events]\n    * @memberof MapData\n    * */\n    events?: (MapEventDefinition | MapEventPlacement)[],\n\n    /** \n     * The sounds that will be played when the map is open. Sounds must be defined on the client side. Then, put the name of the sound identifier\n     * \n     * So, it is possible to play several sounds (in loop or not) on the card. You can put a background music (BGM) and a background noise (BGS) for example\n     * \n     *  ```ts\n     * sounds: ['my-bgm', 'my-bgs']\n     * ```\n     * \n     * And client side:\n     * \n     * ```ts\n     * import { Sound } from '@rpgjs/client'\n     * \n     * @Sound({\n     *      sounds: {\n     *          'my-bgm': require('./assets/bgm.ogg'),\n     *          'my-bgs': require('./assets/bgs.ogg')\n     *      },\n     *      loop: true\n     * })\n     * export class Sounds {}\n     * ```\n     * \n     * See [https://docs.rpgjs.dev/classes/sound.html#properties](RpgSound Decorator)\n     * \n     * @prop {Array<string>} [sounds]\n     * @memberof MapData\n     * */\n    sounds?: string[]\n\n    /**\n     * Initial weather state for this map.\n     *\n     * This value is applied when the map is loaded and can later be updated\n     * at runtime with `map.setWeather()` from server logic.\n     *\n     * ```ts\n     * @MapData({\n     *   id: 'forest',\n     *   file: require('./tmx/forest.tmx'),\n     *   weather: {\n     *     effect: 'fog',\n     *     preset: 'rpgForestFog',\n     *     params: { density: 1.2, height: 0.75 },\n     *     transitionMs: 1200\n     *   }\n     * })\n     * class ForestMap extends RpgMap {}\n     * ```\n     */\n    weather?: WeatherState | null\n\n    /** \n     * Whether to stop all sounds before playing the map sounds when a player joins.\n     * \n     * If set to `true`, all currently playing sounds will be stopped before playing the new map sounds.\n     * This prevents sound overlap when changing maps.\n     * \n     * By default, this is `false`, meaning sounds from the previous map will continue playing.\n     * \n     * ```ts\n     * @MapData({\n     *     id: 'battle-map',\n     *     sounds: ['battle-theme'],\n     *     stopAllSoundsBeforeJoin: true // Stop all sounds before playing battle theme\n     * })\n     * class BattleMap extends RpgMap {}\n     * ```\n     * \n     * @prop {boolean} [stopAllSoundsBeforeJoin=false]\n     * @memberof MapData\n     * @since 5.0.0\n     * */\n    stopAllSoundsBeforeJoin?: boolean\n\n    /** \n     * Specify which properties will be synchronized with the client. On the client side, you can retrieve the values synchronized with the valueChanges property on the scene\n     * \n     * You must create the schema:\n     * \n     * ```ts\n     * import { MapData, RpgMap } from '@rpgjs/server'\n     * \n     * @MapData({\n     *      id: 'medieval',\n     *      file: require('./tmx/town.tmx'),\n     *      syncSchema: {\n     *          count: Number\n     *      }\n     * })\n     * export class TownMap extends RpgMap {\n     *      count: number = 0\n     * \n     *      onLoad() {}\n     * \n     *      onJoin() {\n     *          this.count++\n     *      }\n     * \n     *      onLeave(player) {\n     *          super.onLeave(player)\n     *          this.count--\n     *      }\n     * }\n     * \n     * ```\n     * \n     * If you want to change the scheme of players and events, consider overwriting the existing scheme\n     * \n     *  ```ts\n     * import { MapData, RpgMap, RpgPlayer } from '@rpgjs/server'\n     * \n     * \n     * declare module '@rpgjs/server' {\n     *  export interface RpgPlayer {\n     *      customProp: string\n     *  }\n     * }\n     * \n     * @MapData({\n     *      id: 'medieval',\n     *      file: require('./tmx/town.tmx'),\n     *      syncSchema: {\n     *          users: [\n     *              {\n     *                  customProp: String,\n     *                  ...RpgPlayer.schemas\n     *              }\n     *          ]\n     *      }\n     * })\n     * export class TownMap extends RpgMap {}\n     * ```\n     * \n     * The properties are called `users` and `events`. Their scheme is identical and defined in `RpgPlayer.schemas`. To write schematics, refer to the [documentation of the simple-room](https://github.com/RSamaium/simple-room) module\n     * \n     * @prop {object} [syncSchema]\n     * @memberof MapData\n     * */\n    syncSchema?: any\n\n    /** \n     * Decreases the RAM of the map. In this case, some instructions will be different.\n     * \n     * `map.getTileByIndex()` will not return all tiles of an index but only the tile of the highest layer\n     * \n     * > You can also use the `low-memory` property in Tiled maps\n     * \n     * @prop {boolean} [lowMemory=false]\n     * @since 3.1.0\n     * @memberof MapData\n     * */\n    lowMemory?: boolean\n\n    /** \n     * Called when the map is loaded and initialized\n     * \n     * This hook is executed once when the map data is loaded and ready.\n     * Use this to initialize map-specific properties or setup.\n     * \n     * @prop { () => any } [onLoad]\n     * @memberof MapData\n     * @example\n     * ```ts\n     * @MapData({\n     *     id: 'town',\n     *     file: require('./tmx/town.tmx'),\n     *     onLoad() {\n     *         console.log('Town map loaded')\n     *         // Initialize map properties\n     *     }\n     * })\n     * class TownMap extends RpgMap {}\n     * ```\n     * */\n    onLoad?: () => any\n\n    /** \n     * Called when a player joins the map\n     * \n     * This hook is executed each time a player joins the map.\n     * Use this to perform actions when a player enters the map.\n     * \n     * @prop { (player: RpgPlayer) => any } [onJoin]\n     * @memberof MapData\n     * @example\n     * ```ts\n     * @MapData({\n     *     id: 'town',\n     *     file: require('./tmx/town.tmx'),\n     *     onJoin(player: RpgPlayer) {\n     *         console.log(`${player.name} joined the town`)\n     *         // Perform actions when player joins\n     *     }\n     * })\n     * class TownMap extends RpgMap {}\n     * ```\n     * */\n    onJoin?: (player: RpgPlayer) => any\n\n    /** \n     * Called when a player leaves the map\n     * \n     * This hook is executed each time a player leaves the map.\n     * Use this to perform cleanup or actions when a player exits the map.\n     * \n     * @prop { (player: RpgPlayer) => any } [onLeave]\n     * @memberof MapData\n     * @example\n     * ```ts\n     * @MapData({\n     *     id: 'town',\n     *     file: require('./tmx/town.tmx'),\n     *     onLeave(player: RpgPlayer) {\n     *         console.log(`${player.name} left the town`)\n     *         // Perform cleanup when player leaves\n     *     }\n     * })\n     * class TownMap extends RpgMap {}\n     * ```\n     * */\n    onLeave?: (player: RpgPlayer) => any\n}\n\nexport function MapData(options: MapOptions) {\n    return (target) => {\n        target.file = options.file\n        target.id = options.id\n        target.type = 'map'\n        target.prototype.name = options.name\n        target.prototype.file = options.file\n        target.prototype.id = options.id\n        target.prototype.sounds = options.sounds\n        target.prototype.weather = options.weather\n        target.prototype.lowMemory = options.lowMemory\n        target.prototype.stopAllSoundsBeforeJoin = options.stopAllSoundsBeforeJoin\n\n        target.prototype.$schema = {}\n\n        if (options.syncSchema) {\n            target.prototype.$schema = options.syncSchema\n        }\n        target.prototype._events = options.events\n        \n
